summary: README.md needs full rewrite
description: README.md is significantly out of date. Says six tutorial chapters complete (there are 13), roadmap tables show kernel primitives and fc.py features as not-started when most are done, references getting-started/ directory which is actually docs/, and the status section doesn't reflect current project state.

summary: forth/kernel/README.md primitives table incomplete
description: The primitives table stops at the early set (DUP, DROP, SWAP, OVER, +, -, @, !, EMIT, CR, HALT). Missing all primitives added for chapters 6-12: DO, LOOP, I, *, /MOD, KEY, 0BRANCH, BRANCH, =, <>, <, >, 0=, AT. Memory layout also shows an old START address.

summary: forth/tools/README.md shows completed features as not-yet
description: The 'What fc.py does not do' table still lists IF/THEN/LOOP as not yet implemented — they are done. The compilation pipeline description omits DO/LOOP, IF/ELSE/THEN, BEGIN/AGAIN/UNTIL. Uses CLOADM which should be LOADM.

summary: Bare Naked kernel: replace POLCAT with direct PIA0 keyboard scan
description: CODE_KEY (line 350 of forth/kernel/kernel.asm) is the kernel's only ROM dependency, calling JSR [$A000] (POLCAT via Extended Color BASIC ROM). Replacing this with direct PIA0 access makes the kernel fully ROM-independent and ready for cartridge delivery without BASIC loaded. Work includes: (1) new KBD-SCAN primitive writing col_mask to PIA0-B ($FF02) and reading PIA0-A ($FF00); (2) rewritten CODE_KEY with register-based debounce and MATRIX2ASCII lookup table; (3) AND and OR primitives; (4) KEY-HELD? Forth word; (5) optional KEY? non-blocking variant. Also requires fc.py word table updates for kbd-scan, and, or.

summary: AND / OR bitwise primitives
description: Add CODE_AND ( n1 n2 -- n ) and CODE_OR ( n1 n2 -- n ) to kernel.asm (CFA table entries + implementations). Add 'and' and 'or' to fc.py kernel_words dict. These are needed by KEY-HELD? and general bitwise application code.

summary: PIA0-A direction register init in START
description: Add bare-metal PIA0 initialisation to the START routine in kernel.asm: write to PIA0-A control register ($FF01) to set port A as input and enable the data register, and write to PIA0-B control register ($FF03) to set port B as output. BASIC does this at boot; a bare-metal cartridge must do it explicitly. Prerequisite for KBD-SCAN and the new CODE_KEY.

summary: KBD-SCAN primitive ( col_mask -- row_bits )
description: Add CODE_KBD_SCAN to kernel.asm: write col_mask to PIA0-B ($FF02), read PIA0-A ($FF00), invert, mask bit 7 (joystick comparator), push result. Passing $00 strobes all columns simultaneously for a fast any-key check. Add 'kbd-scan' to fc.py kernel_words. Depends on issue 6 (PIA0 init).

summary: MATRIX2ASCII table + CODE_KEY rewrite
description: Replace CODE_KEY's JSR [$A000] / POLCAT call with a direct PIA0 scan loop. Includes: (1) register-based two-poll debounce (no RAM needed); (2) MATRIX2ASCII BSR subroutine with a 56-entry lookup table (7 cols x 8 rows) mapping matrix position to ASCII; (3) conditional assembly (IFDEF COCO3) for CoCo 3 variant table covering F1/F2/ALT. Eliminates the last ROM dependency. Depends on issues 6 and 7.

summary: Add INCLUDE directive to fc.py
description: Implement INCLUDE <filename> as a compile-time host directive in fc.py. When the tokeniser encounters INCLUDE, it reads the named file (relative to the source file's directory) and splices its tokens into the stream before continuing. Enables a lib/ directory of shared Forth source. Standard ANS Forth FILE word set behaviour, implemented as a cross-compiler host operation.

summary: KEY-HELD? word and KB-Cx constants in lib/keyboard.fs
description: Create forth/lib/keyboard.fs containing: column mask constants (KB-C0 through KB-C6) as Forth CONSTANT definitions, and KEY-HELD? ( col_mask row_bit -- flag ) defined in pure Forth as: SWAP KBD-SCAN AND. Depends on issues 5 (AND), 7 (KBD-SCAN), and 9 (INCLUDE). Applications include it with: INCLUDE lib/keyboard.fs

summary: KEY? non-blocking variant ( -- char|0 )
description: Add CODE_KEY_NB to kernel.asm: same PIA0 scan as CODE_KEY but returns 0 immediately if no key is pressed rather than spinning. Useful for game loops, timeouts, and polling. Add 'key?' to fc.py kernel_words. Depends on issue 8 (MATRIX2ASCII table, which KEY? will share via BSR).

summary: Typewriter test app in src/typewriter
description: Create src/typewriter/typewriter.fs: a simple app that calls KEY in a loop and EMITs each character to the screen. Tests the new PIA0-based KEY implementation end-to-end. ENTER advances to the next row (CR), CLEAR resets cursor to top-left. Includes cursor display, arrow key movement, backspace, SHIFT support, and VSYNC-based auto-repeat.

summary: Demo: Snake game
description: Classic snake game using VDG semigraphics-4 color blocks. Arrow keys control direction, snake grows when eating food, game over on wall/self collision. Exercises keyboard input, game loop timing (VSYNC), screen drawing, and game state management.

summary: Demo: Drawing program
description: Arrow keys move a brush cursor, number keys select colors. Uses VDG semigraphics modes for a 64x32 color pixel canvas. Demonstrates semigraphics block characters, color selection, and interactive screen painting.

summary: Demo: Sound/music player
description: Explore the CoCo's 6-bit DAC on PIA1. Simple tone generator or melody player. Could play a sequence of notes or allow interactive keyboard-to-tone mapping (piano). Opens up PIA1 hardware access.

summary: Demo: Conway's Game of Life
description: Cellular automaton on the VDG semigraphics grid. Visually satisfying and a good stress test for Forth performance on the 6809. Could use semigraphics-4 for color or text mode for simplicity.

summary: Demo: Starfield
description: Stars flying toward the viewer, simulating depth with different speeds. Pure VDG semigraphics, no math library needed. VSYNC-timed animation loop.

summary: Demo: Clock / stopwatch
description: Big digit display using block characters, ticks off the VSYNC 60Hz counter. Could include a stopwatch mode with start/stop/reset. Demonstrates timed display updates and large character rendering.

summary: Demo: Maze generator and navigator
description: Random recursive backtracker draws maze walls in real time using semigraphics, then lets the player navigate with arrow keys. Requires a random number generator (could seed from VSYNC timing or keyboard).

summary: Demo: Text adventure
description: Navigate between rooms with N/S/E/W commands. Room descriptions, items to pick up, simple puzzles. Pushes the Forth toolchain into string handling, data tables for room connections, and state tracking. A CoCo classic.

summary: Demo: Sprite animation
description: Walking character or bouncing ball using VDG semigraphics. Explores smooth movement, erase-draw cycles, and VSYNC-timed animation on the CoCo's character-mapped display.

summary: Tool: Memory monitor / hex editor
description: Hex dump display, live memory editor, peek/poke any address. Arrow keys navigate, hex input to modify bytes. Actually useful as a development tool for inspecting kernel state, variables, and screen RAM.

summary: Demo: Reaction time game
description: Wait for a random delay, flash the screen, measure how fast the player hits a key. Uses VSYNC frame counter for timing. Simple but fun, and demonstrates precise timing measurement.

summary: Demo: Serial terminal
description: Hook up the CoCo's serial port (PIA1 bit-banged or cartridge slot UART) and make a dumb terminal. Most ambitious hardware-wise — connects the CoCo to the outside world. Practical for DriveWire or other serial communication.

summary: Tool: 6809 disassembler
description: Disassemble memory regions into 6809 mnemonics. Takes a start address and length, displays address, hex bytes, and decoded instruction. Must handle variable-length instructions (1-5 bytes), $10/$11 prefix pages, and indexed addressing postbyte decoding. Big but highly useful for inspecting compiled Forth output and kernel code on-device.

summary: Tool: Stack viewer
description: Display data stack and return stack contents on screen. Show stack depth and values in hex. Small enough to be a library word — no kernel changes needed. Immediately useful for debugging any Forth program. Could be invoked with a word like .S (print stack).

summary: Tool: Word lister
description: Show all compiled words with their CFA addresses. Helps verify that fc.py produced what you expected. Could display kernel primitives and app-defined words with their entry points. Requires fc.py to emit a word table or symbol list into the binary.

summary: Tool: Breakpoint trap
description: A Forth word (e.g. BRK or DEBUG) that can be inserted anywhere in Forth code. When executed, dumps registers and stack contents to the screen and waits for a keypress before continuing. Like a printf-debugger for Forth. Implementable as a library word using existing primitives.

summary: Tool: Memory compare
description: Diff two memory regions byte-by-byte, highlighting differences. Takes two addresses and a length. Useful for verifying binaries match expectations or checking if RAM has been corrupted. Display shows address, expected byte, actual byte for each mismatch.

summary: Tool: Execution tracer
description: Single-step through the Forth thread, showing each word as it executes with stack state. The most powerful debugging tool but also the hardest — requires hooking DOCOL and NEXT in the kernel to intercept each threaded dispatch. Could show CFA address, word name (if symbol table available), and stack contents at each step.

summary: Lib: Hi-res graphics (PMODE)
description: Closed: VDG mode switching exists in vdg.fs (set-sam-v, set-sam-f, set-pia, reset-text). No unified pmode abstraction needed — separate per-mode libraries (rg-pixel.fs for RG6, future cg-pixel.fs for CG modes) conserve app space by only loading what each app uses.

summary: Lib: Pixel plot and line drawing
description: Closed: Pixel plot and line drawing exist in rg-pixel.fs as optimized CODE words (rg-pset, rg-pget, rg-line, rg-hline). Future CG-mode equivalents would go in a separate cg-pixel.fs library.

summary: Lib: Circle drawing
description: CIRCLE ( cx cy r color -- ) draws a circle using the midpoint circle algorithm. Optional filled variant. Extended Color BASIC's CIRCLE also supports aspect ratio and arc segments — consider a simplified subset. Depends on issue 32 (pixel/line drawing).

summary: Lib: Flood fill (PAINT)
description: PAINT ( x y fill-color border-color -- ) flood-fills a bounded region. Extended Color BASIC uses a scanline seed fill. On the 6809 with limited RAM, a simple stack-based fill may hit memory limits — consider a scanline approach. Depends on issue 32.

summary: Lib: DRAW turtle graphics
description: A DRAW-like command inspired by Extended Color BASIC's DRAW string language. Compact command strings like 'U10R5D10L5' to draw shapes. Commands: U/D/L/R (up/down/left/right), E/F/G/H (diagonals), M (move to), B (blank move), N (no cursor update), A (angle 0-3), S (scale), C (color). Extremely efficient for defining complex shapes in minimal code. Implement as a Forth word that interprets a command string stored in memory.

summary: Lib: Sound and DAC access
description: SOUND ( pitch duration -- ) generates a tone via the CoCo's 6-bit DAC on PIA1 ($FF20 bits 2-7). Pitch controls frequency by toggling the DAC output in a timed loop, duration controls length. Also expose DAC-OUT ( value -- ) for direct 6-bit DAC writes (values 0-63 shifted left 2 into $FF20). Foundation for all sound/music features.

summary: Lib: PLAY music command
description: A PLAY-like command inspired by Extended Color BASIC's PLAY string language. Compact music notation like 'T5O3CDEFGAB' to play melodies. Commands: A-G (notes), O (octave 1-5), L (note length), T (tempo 1-31), P (pause), # (sharp). Extremely efficient for encoding music in minimal code. Uses the DAC (issue 36) for tone generation. Implement as a Forth word that interprets a music string stored in memory.

summary: Demo: Joy to the World (music + graphics)
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's sample program. Plays 'Joy to the World' melody while drawing colorful graphics on screen. Combines the PLAY music library (issue 37) with PMODE hi-res graphics (issue 31). A showcase of audio-visual capability.

summary: Demo: Animated rocket
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's animated rocket program. Uses GET/PUT-style sprite blitting to move a rocket shape across a hi-res graphics screen. Demonstrates sprite animation, erase-draw cycles, and PMODE graphics. Depends on issues 31-32 (graphics library).

summary: Demo: Hi-res font renderer
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's custom character program. Renders text characters in PMODE hi-res graphics using pixel-level font definitions. Shows how to display readable text on a graphics screen where the normal VDG text mode is unavailable. Depends on issues 31-32.

summary: Demo: Back to Bach (music player)
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's Bach Invention program. Plays a classical piece using the PLAY music library. Demonstrates complex musical notation, tempo changes, and sustained performance. A pure audio demo showcasing the DAC sound system. Depends on issue 37 (PLAY library).

summary: Demo: Electronic piano
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's electronic piano. Maps keyboard keys to musical tones — press A through K to play notes from middle C up one octave. Visual feedback shows which note is playing. A simple but satisfying interactive demo. Depends on issue 36 (sound library).

summary: Demo: Kaleidoscope
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's kaleidoscope program. Draws symmetric random patterns using semigraphics-4 SET/RESET. Four-way symmetry creates mesmerizing visual effects. Uses random colors and positions with VSYNC timing. A classic CoCo demo that works with existing semigraphics capabilities — no PMODE needed.

summary: Demo: Ready Aim Fire (shooting game)
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's Ready Aim Fire game. Move a cannon left/right along the bottom of the screen, fire at targets. Text-mode semigraphics with sound effects. Exercises keyboard input, animation, collision detection, and scoring.

summary: Demo: Typing test
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's typing test program. Displays a phrase, times the user as they type it, calculates words per minute. Uses VSYNC frame counter for accurate timing. Counts errors by comparing input character-by-character. A practical demo that exercises KEY?, screen output, and timing.

summary: Demo: Music composer
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's music composer program. Generates random melodies with user-selectable length and tempo. Uses random note selection from a scale, with color screen changes on each note. A generative art/music piece. Depends on issue 36 (sound library).

summary: Demo: Bouncing ball
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's bouncing dot program. A ball bounces around the screen, reflecting off walls. Uses semigraphics-4 SET/RESET for smooth-looking animation. Could evolve into a simple Pong or Breakout. VSYNC-timed for consistent speed.

summary: Demo: Dice/Craps game
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's dice and craps programs. Simulates rolling two dice with ASCII art dice faces on screen. Implements full craps rules: 7/11 on first roll wins, 2/3/12 loses, otherwise roll for point. Sound effects for rolls and results. Needs a random number generator (VSYNC-seeded).

summary: Demo: Speed reading trainer
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's speed reading program. Displays text one line at a time at a user-selected words-per-minute rate. Could use a stored passage (e.g. opening of a novel). Tests how fast you can read and comprehend. VSYNC-timed display updates.

summary: Demo: Math quiz (timed)
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's arithmetic and 'Beat the Computer' programs. Generates random math problems (addition, subtraction, maybe multiplication). Player must answer before a timer runs out. Tracks score and percentage correct. Sound feedback for right/wrong answers. VSYNC timing for countdown display.

summary: VDG mode switching library (forth/lib/vdg.fs)
description: Reusable library for controlling the MC6847 VDG and SAM chip display modes. Words: set-sam-v ( v -- ) sets SAM V0-V2 display mode bits; set-sam-f ( offset -- ) sets SAM F0-F6 display offset; set-pia ( bits -- ) sets PIA $FF22 bits 7-3 preserving 2-0; reset-text ( -- ) restores default Alpha text mode. Encapsulates all the fiddly SAM/PIA register poking so graphics programs don't have to.

summary: 5x7 bitmap font library (forth/lib/font5x7.fs)
description: Reusable 5x7 pixel font for rendering text in graphics modes. 37 glyphs: space, A-Z, 0-9. 7 bytes per glyph with pixels in bits 7-3. Written to $6000 at init using fb pattern from Tetris. glyph-addr ( char -- addr ) maps ASCII to font data address. Reusable by any program needing text in non-Alpha VDG modes.

summary: Demo: VDG Mode Demo (src/vdg-modes/)
description: Cycles through all 11 MC6847 display modes: Alpha Internal, SG4, SG6, CG1-CG6, RG1-RG6. Mode table at $5800: 11 entries x 16 bytes with VRAM base, size, PIA/SAM values, mode name. Text modes write VDG chars directly; RG modes use font byte per row; CG modes nibble-expand font. Each mode shows a visual pattern (color bars, stripes, checkerboard) plus the mode name. Key press advances to next mode; loops forever. Depends on #51 (vdg.fs) and #52 (font5x7.fs).

summary: Demo: Digital Rain (Matrix effect)
description: Matrix-style falling green characters on black VDG text screen. 32 independent columns with random speeds, bright inverse-video heads and dim normal trails. Trail mutation and random spawning create an organic feel. SAM double-speed for fast updates. Uses rng.fs and screen.fs libraries.

summary: Demo: Tetris
description: Full SG4 semigraphics Tetris. 10x16 board, 7 standard pieces with rotation, gravity with speed-up, line clearing, scoring, next-piece preview, high score table. Zero-flicker draw-first-clean-stale rendering. Arrow keys move/rotate, space for hard drop. Key repeat with DAS timing. Uses rng.fs, screen.fs, print.fs, datawrite.fs libraries.

summary: Lib: RG6 artifact-color pixel primitives (rg-pixel.fs)
description: Pixel primitives for RG6 mode (PMODE 4) with NTSC artifact coloring. Treats pixel pairs as artifact-color units (00=black, 11=white, 01=blue, 10=red) at effective 128x192 resolution. Words: rg-pset ( x y color -- ) plot artifact pixel, rg-pget ( x y -- color ) read pixel, rg-pcls ( -- ) clear to black, rg-hline ( x1 x2 y color -- ) fast horizontal line. VRAM at $3000, 6144 bytes, 32 bytes/row. Foundation for all Space Warp graphics.

summary: Lib: Bresenham line drawing for RG6 (rg-pixel.fs)
description: Add rg-line ( x1 y1 x2 y2 color -- ) to rg-pixel.fs using Bresenham's algorithm. Draws lines in artifact color for maser beams, enemy fire, and tactical display borders. Must handle all octants correctly. Depends on #56.

summary: Lib: Software sprite library (sprite.fs)
description: Software sprites for RG6 artifact-color mode. Sprite data format: width, height, then row bytes (2bpp artifact-color packed). spr-draw ( addr x y -- ) draws sprite with color 0 as transparent. spr-erase ( addr x y -- ) erases sprite footprint to black. Background save/restore for clean overdraw without flicker. Used for Endever, Jovians, bases, stars, triton missile ball. Depends on #56.

summary: RG6 artifact graphics test app (src/rg-test/)
description: Test application for RG6 artifact-color graphics. Exercises pixel plot, line draw, sprite draw/erase, and trig-based line angles. Fixed spr-erase→spr-erase-box rename. All tests passing at various angles, sprite draw/erase, and text rendering. Visual verification via XRoar with NTSC artifact display. Depends on #56-58.

summary: Lib: Sine/cosine lookup table for angle-based firing
description: 8-bit sine/cosine lookup table for converting firing angles (0-360 degrees) to dx/dy vectors. 91-entry table (0-90 degrees) with quadrant mirroring. Fixed-point values scaled for tactical view dimensions. Used by masers and triton missiles for angle-to-direction conversion.

summary: Space Warp: galaxy generation (8x8 grid, random placement)
description: Galaxy generation implemented: 8x8 grid with random placement of stars, Jovians, bases, black holes, and storms based on difficulty level. Quadrant packing, expansion, and rendering all working. Long range scan (command 3) still open as separate issue #68.

summary: Space Warp: tactical view rendering (border, stars, sprites)
description: Render the tactical quadrant view in the upper 128x144 artifact pixels. Draw border, place star/planet dots in random artifact colors, draw sprites for Endever (blue), Jovians (red), bases (blue). Refresh loop redraws objects at their current positions. Depends on #56-58.

summary: Space Warp: status panel rendering (RG6 text)
description: Render the status panel in the lower 128x48 artifact pixels (rows 144-191). Display STARDATE, QUADRANT, ENERGY, DEFLECTORS, MISSILES, CONDITION, SOS alerts, and COMMAND? prompt. Update values each frame. Uses rg-text.fs for white text on black background.

summary: Space Warp: real-time arrow key movement
description: Arrow keys move the Endever within the tactical view using ion engines. Movement is continuous while held and works concurrently with command input. Uses KEY? polling each frame. Movement speed affected by ion engine damage. Consumes small amount of ship energy. Depends on #62.

summary: Space Warp: command input system (number keys + params)
description: Command dispatch: number keys 1-7 trigger commands. Some require additional numeric input (angles 0-360, coordinates 0-7, energy 0-100). Input is entered digit-by-digit while the game loop continues (arrow keys still work, Jovians still fire). Display typed digits in status panel. Depends on #63.

summary: Space Warp: masers (command 5 — angle fire, blue beam)
description: Press 5, enter angle 0-360. Draw blue line from Endever at specified angle across tactical view. Line stops when hitting a Jovian (deal damage based on distance and shield level), a star/black hole (blocked), or screen edge (miss). Maser power reduced by deflector setting and maser damage. Consumes energy. Depends on #57, #60, #65.

summary: Space Warp: triton missiles (command 6 — red fireball)
description: Press 6, enter angle 0-360. Animate a red fireball moving from Endever along the firing angle. One-hit kill on Jovian contact. Limited supply (start with 10, replenished by docking). Two alternating sprites (+/x) create spinning fireball look. 100px/frame speed, spawns outside ship sprite boundary. Costs 5 energy. Depends on #58, #60, #65.

summary: Space Warp: long range scan (command 3 — galaxy map)
description: Press 3 to replace tactical view with 8x8 galaxy map. Show E=Endever, B=base, 1-3=Jovian count, M=magnetic storm per quadrant. Column/row headers 0-7. Data reflects last-known state (goes stale as Jovians move). Damaged scanners show garbled/missing data. Press any key to return. Depends on #61, #65.

summary: Space Warp: damage report (command 1)
description: Press 1 to show full-screen damage report. Display Jovians left, bases left, and operational percentage for all 5 systems (ion engines, hyperdrive, scanners, deflectors, masers). Press any key to return to tactical view. Depends on #65.

summary: Space Warp: hyperdrive (command 2 — warp between quadrants)
description: Press 2, enter 2-digit destination (col 0-7, row 0-7). Warp to new quadrant. Energy cost = distance*2, capped at 10. Damaged hyperdrive may mis-jump. Safe spawn avoids gravity wells. Cancels beams/missiles, redraws tactical view.

summary: Space Warp: deflectors (command 4 — shield energy setting)
description: Press 4, enter 0-100 to set shield percentage. Higher shields absorb more damage but reduce maser power (50%=1/3 loss, 100%=2/3 loss). Maximum capped by deflector system damage. Setting persists until changed. Depends on #65.

summary: Space Warp: self-destruct (command 7)
description: Press 7, confirm with code 123 + ENTER. Async countdown 5→1 (game loop continues), cancel with 7123. Biggest explosion (radius 8→68, 48 dots/frame, 20 frames), 200 proximity damage in 60px radius. Chain-explodes nearby Jovians. Sets death-cause=2 to skip redundant death explosion.

summary: Space Warp: energy model (depletion, recharge)
description: Ship energy percentage depleted by: movement (small), maser fire (moderate), damage taken (variable, reduced by shields). Energy reaches 0% = ship destroyed. Recharged to 100% by docking. Status panel shows current energy. Depends on #63.

summary: Space Warp: ship damage system (5 systems, degradation)
description: Five ship systems with 0-100% operational level. Jovian hits deal 20 damage to random system. Ion engines: 3-tier speed. Hyperdrive: mis-jump at <50%. Masers: damage scales linearly. Docking repairs +2/tick. Scanners and deflectors take damage but depend on #68 and #71 for gameplay effects.

summary: Space Warp: Jovian AI (movement, firing, targeting)
description: Jovian AI: Manhattan chase toward player, obstacle avoidance (stars 6px, black holes 15px, bases 5px), speed scaling with difficulty. Red beam firing at player. Gravity from black holes and stars pulls/kills Jovians. State machine (ATTACK/FLEE/IDLE) deferred to #114.

summary: Space Warp: docking (repair, resupply at bases)
description: Fly Endever directly above or below a base to dock. Status shows ENDEVER DOCKED, condition changes to DOCKED. Repairs all 5 systems to 100%, restores energy to 100%, replenishes triton missiles to 10. Stardates continue during docking. Depends on #64, #73, #74.

summary: Space Warp: SOS alerts (base under attack warnings)
description: Off-screen base threat detection. Each stardate, scan galaxy for quadrants with both Jovians and a base. Flash SOS-BASE col,row in status panel. 1-in-8 chance per stardate of base destruction.

summary: Space Warp: black holes (invisible hazard, shot blocking)
description: Black holes occupy positions in quadrants but are invisible on the tactical display. Maser beams and triton missiles that hit a black hole disappear silently. Flying the Endever into a black hole destroys it instantly. Player must deduce positions from blocked shots. Depends on #62, #66, #67. Implemented: collision (instant death with BLACK HOLE message), 30px gravity well with tiered pull rates, ship vanishes on contact. Remaining: shot blocking (masers/missiles absorbed by black hole).

summary: Space Warp: magnetic storms (scanner blocking)
description: Magnetic storms block scanner readings in affected quadrants. Long range scan shows M instead of contents. Player must warp into a storm quadrant to discover what's there. May also interfere with hyperdrive navigation. Depends on #68.

summary: Space Warp: scoring and difficulty levels
description: Closed: replaced by #200 (end-game rating). No running score needed.

summary: Space Warp: win/lose conditions and game over screen
description: Win: all Jovians destroyed + at least 1 base survives. Lose: all bases destroyed, Endever destroyed (0% energy or black hole), or self-destruct. Display final score and CARE TO PLAY AGAIN? prompt. Restart on keypress. Depends on #80.

summary: Space Warp: title screen and mission briefing
description: Title screen with SPACE WARP banner and difficulty selection (1-9). Mission briefing shows fleet size, base count, and stardate limit. Both implemented and tested.

summary: Space Warp: maser fire sound effect
description: Sound effect for maser beam firing. Requires sound/DAC library (issue #36). Future enhancement.

summary: Space Warp: triton missile launch sound
description: Sound effect for triton missile launch and travel. Requires sound/DAC library (issue #36). Future enhancement.

summary: Space Warp: explosion sound effect
description: Sound effect for Jovian ship destruction and other explosions. Requires sound/DAC library (issue #36). Future enhancement.

summary: Space Warp: docking sound
description: Sound effect for docking at a UP base. Requires sound/DAC library (issue #36). Future enhancement.

summary: Space Warp: SOS alarm sound
description: Audio alarm when SOS-BASE alert is active. Requires sound/DAC library (issue #36). Future enhancement.

summary: Space Warp: self-destruct countdown sound
description: Dramatic countdown sound/tone sequence during self-destruct confirmation. Requires sound/DAC library (issue #36). Future enhancement.

summary: Space Warp: star twinkling animation
description: Randomly toggle star pixel patterns between artifact colors each frame for a living starfield effect. Stars shift between blue/red/white bit pairs. Subtle visual polish. Future enhancement. Depends on #62.

summary: Space Warp: high score table
description: Persist high scores across game restarts, similar to Tetris implementation. Display top 5 scores with difficulty level. Show on game over screen. Future enhancement. Depends on #81.

summary: Space Warp: maser hit detection and Jovian damage
description: After drawing a maser beam, check if any Jovian position lies within a few pixels of the beam path. Deal damage based on distance (closer = more damage) and shield level (higher shields = weaker masers). Reduce Jovian health in JOV-DMG array. When health reaches 0, destroy the Jovian: erase its sprite, decrement qjovians, update galaxy byte. Depends on #66.

summary: Space Warp: maser beam erase after delay
description: After drawing a maser beam, wait 10-15 frames then erase it by redrawing the same line in black (color 0). Must restore any stars or border pixels the beam crossed. Consider redrawing affected stars after erase. Depends on #66.

summary: Optimize graphics primitives as kernel assembly
description: Move rg-pset, rg-line (Bresenham), spr-draw, spr-erase-box, and rg-char to 6809 assembly kernel primitives. ITC Forth overhead makes these ~30x slower than native. rg-pset alone is called per-pixel by lines, sprites, and fonts. Also page out ROMs ($FFDE) to reclaim $8000-$FEFF as RAM. Expected gains: rg-line from ~1500 to ~40 cycles/pixel, spr-draw from ~800 to ~50 cycles/pixel.

summary: fc.py: tokenizer captures CODE...;CODE blocks as raw asm text
fc.py: parser builds code_definitions from CODE tokens
fc.py: preprocess_asm + assemble_code_words via lwasm
fc.py: compiler integrates CODE words into binary layout
CODE word end-to-end test app
Migrate 5 graphics primitives from kernel to library CODE words
description: Moved rg-pset, rg-line, spr-draw, spr-erase-box, rg-char from kernel.asm to CODE words in their respective libraries (rg-pixel.fs, sprite.fs, rg-text.fs). Freed ~652 bytes of kernel ROM. Scratch variables remain in kernel RAM at $0050+ for CODE word EQU resolution.

summary: Font-art.fs: digit 7 looks like letter T
description: The 7 glyph in font-art.fs used centered stem (.#. / $30) for all rows below the top bar, making it identical to T. Fixed by shifting top two stem rows right (..# / $0C) to create a diagonal descent.

summary: Space Warp: star collision (fly into star = destroyed)
description: Flying the Endever within 3px of a star destroys the ship (energy set to 0). Collision check runs after both player movement and gravity pulls.

summary: Space Warp: gravity wells (black holes and stars)
description: Black holes have a 30px gravity well with tiered pull: 20-30px every 4 frames, 10-20px every 2 frames, 6-10px every frame, <6px 2px/frame (inescapable). Stars have a weaker 10px well: <5px every 2 frames, 5-10px every 4 frames, always 1px pull. Adds tactical depth — navigate carefully around celestial objects.

summary: Space Warp: redraw stars/base/jovians after ship erase
description: Ship bounding-box erase was wiping out stars, base, and jovian sprites when the ship moved through their positions. Fixed by redrawing all objects between erase-ship and draw-ship in the game loop.

summary: Space Warp: flicker-free ship movement via background save/restore
description: bg-save and bg-restore CODE words save/restore 3x5 VRAM bytes under the ship sprite. Eliminates black flash on ship erase — background is instantly restored instead of cleared to black then redrawn. 15 bytes saved per sprite position.

summary: Space Warp: flicker-free triton missiles via background save/restore
description: Reuse bg-save/bg-restore (now parameterized with buffer address) for missile sprites. Separate MSL-BG buffer at $6AE0. Missiles no longer leave black rectangles when moving or hitting targets.

summary: Space Warp: magnetic storm fake stars + black hole spiral
description: In storm quadrants, scatter 3x fake stars (same colors as real) to obscure which dots are lethal. Black hole gravity well kept clear of fake stars. Blue spiral arms (4 arms, 8 dots each) mark the event horizon — only visible in storm quadrants. Spiral positions precomputed at quadrant entry via plot-dots CODE word for fast redraw.

summary: Space Warp: plot-dots CODE word for bulk pixel plotting
description: Assembly primitive plot-dots ( addr count color -- ) plots count pixels from (x,y) byte pairs at addr, all in one color. Eliminates ITC loop overhead for repeated rg-pset calls. Used for event horizon spiral and storm star redraw.

summary: Move VRAM from $5000 to $5200 for more app space
description: App binary exceeded $5000 (12288 byte limit). Moved VRAM base to $5200, giving 12800 bytes for app code. All game data addresses shifted up by $200 ($6800->$6A00, sprites $6900->$6B00, etc). CODE word buffer addresses updated.

summary: Space Warp: gradual energy recharge while docked
description: When docked at a base, energy should recharge over time on an inverse logarithmic curve — fast when empty, slowing every 20% until reaching 100%. Currently docking instantly sets energy to 100%. The recharge should happen per-frame while docked, making the player choose between waiting for full charge or undocking early.

summary: Space Warp: base collision prevents ship overlap
description: Ship should not be able to fly through bases. Movement blocked within 5px of base center. Docking triggers at 8px (before blocking wall). Ship can approach from any direction, dock, and undock freely.

summary: Jovian AI foundation: VRAM move, data structures, flicker-free rendering
description: Move VRAM from $5400 to $5600 for AI code headroom. Shift all game data +$200. Add AI data structures (JOV-STATE, JOV-TICK, JOV-BG, JOV-OLDX/OLDY). Implement flicker-free Jovian rendering with bg-save/restore.

summary: Jovian AI movement: chase player with obstacle avoidance
description: Basic chase-player AI with 1px/N frames movement speed scaling with difficulty. Obstacle avoidance for stars, black holes, bases. Bounds clamping 4-123 x, 4-139 y.

summary: Jovian AI firing: red beams at player
description: Red beams from Jovians at player. Dedicated jbeam-* variables (separate from maser). Fire cooldown: 150-(level*14) frames, min 24. 5 energy damage per hit. Beam rendered via drawn flags to avoid bg-save artifacts. Tuned for survivability.

summary: Jovian AI state machine: ATTACK/FLEE/IDLE
description: Three-state AI: ATTACK moves toward target and fires, FLEE moves toward nearest star for cover when health drops below threshold, IDLE fires opportunistically. Difficulty scaling of thresholds.

summary: Jovian AI polish: base targeting, collision avoidance, balance
description: Base targeting priority over player (jov-think). Jovian-to-Jovian collision avoidance 8px (#214). Balance: emotion-scaled fire rate, genome tick speed, engagement distance, skill-based star avoidance.

summary: Add UNLOOP kernel primitive
description: UNLOOP ( -- ) (R: loop-sys -- ) removes DO/LOOP control parameters (index and limit) from the return stack without branching. Needed for early exit from loops via EXIT. Without it, EXIT inside a DO/LOOP corrupts the return stack. Implementation: LEAS 4,S then NEXT. Add CFA_UNLOOP to kernel.asm and 'unloop' to fc.py kernel_words(). Standard ANS Forth word.

summary: Add ROT kernel primitive
description: ROT ( a b c -- b c a ) rotates top three stack items. Third most common stack operation after DUP and SWAP. Currently requires >R SWAP R> SWAP (4 words). Assembly: load 3 values from U, store back rotated, ~12 bytes.

summary: Add 2DUP kernel primitive
description: 2DUP ( a b -- a b a b ) duplicates top two stack items. Currently written as OVER OVER. Assembly: LDD ,U / LDY 2,U / STD ,--U / STY ,--U or similar, ~10 bytes.

summary: Add +! kernel primitive
description: +! ( n addr -- ) adds n to the cell at addr. Extremely common for incrementing variables. Currently requires DUP @ ROT + SWAP ! (5 words). Assembly: pop addr and n, load cell, add, store back, ~10 bytes. Applied at 19 sites in spacewarp.fs + 3 in library files.

summary: Add >R R> R@ kernel primitives
description: >R ( n -- ) (R: -- n ) moves TOS to return stack. R> ( -- n ) (R: n -- ) moves return stack TOS to data stack. R@ ( -- n ) (R: n -- n ) copies return stack TOS. 6 bytes each, 24 bytes total kernel cost.

summary: Add 2DROP kernel primitive
description: 2DROP ( a b -- ) drops top two stack items. Currently DROP DROP (2 words, 2 NEXT dispatches). Assembly: LEAU 4,U then NEXT, ~4 bytes. Applied at 9 sites in spacewarp.fs.

summary: Add PICK kernel primitive
description: PICK ( n -- x ) copies the nth stack item to TOS. 0 PICK = DUP, 1 PICK = OVER, etc. General-purpose deep stack access without return stack gymnastics. Assembly: LDD ,U (get n) / ASLB ROLA (n*2) / LDD D,U / STD ,U, ~8 bytes.

summary: Add INVERT kernel primitive
description: INVERT ( n -- ~n ) bitwise complement. Complement to AND/OR. Assembly: LDD ,U / COMA / COMB / STD ,U, ~6 bytes.

summary: Add XOR kernel primitive
description: XOR ( n1 n2 -- n3 ) bitwise exclusive or. Completes the bitwise trio with AND and OR. Assembly: LDD ,U++ / EORA ,U / EORB 1,U / STD ,U, ~8 bytes.

summary: Add MIN and MAX kernel primitives
description: MIN ( n1 n2 -- min ) and MAX ( n1 n2 -- max ). Moved from app-level CODE words to kernel. Saves per-app overhead.

summary: Add J kernel primitive
description: J ( -- n ) pushes the outer loop index in nested DO/LOOPs. The outer index is 4 bytes deep on the return stack (past the inner loop's index and limit). Assembly: LDD 4,S / STD ,--U, ~6 bytes.

summary: Add +LOOP kernel primitive
description: +LOOP ( n -- ) like LOOP but increments index by n instead of 1. Enables counting by arbitrary steps and counting down. Requires signed overflow detection for correct termination across the limit boundary. More complex than LOOP — ~20 bytes.

summary: Add ABS kernel primitive
description: ABS ( n -- |n| ) absolute value. Moved from Forth definition in rg-pixel.fs to kernel CODE word. Assembly: test sign, negate if negative.

summary: Add MOD and / kernel primitives
description: Not needed as separate primitives. /MOD gives both remainder and quotient; use /MOD SWAP DROP for division, /MOD DROP for modulus. Documented in kernel README.

summary: Relocate VRAM to $0600, contiguous app space
description: VRAM moved from $4000 to $0600 (below app). App is now a single contiguous block at $2000 (~24K via CLOADM). No more --hole flag needed. fc.py --hole kept for backward compat but unused.

summary: Relocate kernel to $E000 via all-RAM mode
description: Kernel moved from $1000 to $E000 (ROM 2 region). Bootstrap at $0E00 enables all-RAM (STA $FFDF — NOT $FFDE), copies staged kernel from $1000 to $E000. Stacks at $DE00/$E000. 24K runtime RAM at $8000-$DDFF. Previous attempt failed because $FFDE clears TY (keeps ROM), $FFDF sets TY (all-RAM). XRoar works fine with -ram 64.

summary: Interactive boot animation docs (boot-animation.html)
description: Step-by-step HTML animation showing the DECB load, bootstrap, all-RAM, copy, and START sequence. Shows memory map with VRAM at $0600, 24K loadable app, 24K runtime RAM, kernel at $E000. Navigate with arrow keys or buttons.

summary: Add -ram 64 to all demo Makefiles
description: All demos need -ram 64 for XRoar since kernel is at $E000 (requires SAM all-RAM mode with 64K DRAM). Updated: typewriter, kaleidoscope, tetris, rain, rg-test. spacewarp already had it.

summary: Fix rg-test: spr-erase renamed to spr-erase-box
description: rg-test.fs referenced spr-erase which was renamed to spr-erase-box in an earlier commit. Updated both references. Build and run verified.

summary: Explosion effects: animated expanding ring explosions
description: Animated expanding ring explosions using trig library. Dots accumulate across frames (no per-frame erase), building white→red→blue cloud. Per-entity sizes: Jovian (R2→12, 20 dots, 6 frames), Ship (R3→22, 28 dots, 10 frames), Base (R4→26, 32 dots, 12 frames), Self-destruct (R8→68, 48 dots, 20 frames). Proximity damage with chain explosions via bitmask tracking. Full tactical redraw after kills.

summary: Battle rendering artifact audit
description: Fixed missile trail at 0 degrees (layer ordering), missile-kill cleanup (skip stale bg-restore), explosion death screen artifacts (full tactical redraw after explosion).

summary: Kernel scratch variables for beam system (VAR_BEAM_*)
description: Add ~6 bytes of scratch variables to kernel.asm for the beam-trace/beam-draw-slice/beam-restore-slice CODE words: VAR_BEAM_BUF (2 bytes, path buffer pointer), VAR_BEAM_VRAM (2 bytes, VRAM address scratch), VAR_BEAM_CNT (2 bytes, pixel count scratch).

summary: Beam pixel-save library (forth/lib/beam.fs)
description: New library with three CODE words: beam-trace, beam-draw-slice, beam-restore-slice.

summary: Space Warp: animated traveling bolt (maser + Jovian beam)
description: Animated bolt travels along pre-computed Bresenham path. Fixed-length bolt advances BOLT-SPEED pixels per frame. Beam erase plots black instead of restoring saved pixels. Beams stop at first non-black pixel (line-of-sight obstruction).

summary: Space Warp: bbox hit detection during beam-trace
description: Bounding-box hit detection walks traced path buffer checking each pixel against live Jovian bboxes (7x5). Hit found: record Jovian index, truncate beam at hit pixel. Combined with beam-find-obstacle for line-of-sight obstruction.

summary: Space Warp: revised render loop with pixel-save beams
description: Layered rendering: erase beams first (Jovian before player), sprite bg-save/restore cycle, then advance beam heads. Beams drawn last, erased first. Forces sprite refresh every frame while beams active.

summary: Space Warp: explosion/kill integration with beam cancel
description: When bolt reaches target: cancel beams, spr-erase-box dead Jovian, explode-jovian with animated ring, proximity-damage with chain explosions, refresh-after-kill (full tactical redraw). Damage applied when bolt head reaches hit pixel.

summary: Update spacewarp documentation for new beam system
description: Updated SPEC.md and USERGUIDE.md: explosion system (animated rings, proximity damage, chain explosions), beam line-of-sight (stops at non-black pixels), safe spawn, warp cost, self-destruct sequence.

summary: Warp energy cost reduced and capped
description: Warp cost changed from distance*5 to distance*2, capped at 10 max. Previously a cross-galaxy jump cost 70 energy (most of starting 100), stranding the player after a few jumps.

summary: Safe spawn: ship avoids gravity wells on quadrant entry
description: After expand-quadrant, safe-spawn checks Manhattan distance to all stars and black hole. If ship is within 35px, relocates to random position. Retries up to 16 times. Prevents instant death from spawning in inescapable gravity wells.

summary: Beams stop at non-black pixels (line of sight)
description: beam-find-obstacle scans traced path buffer for first non-black saved pixel and truncates beam there. Beams no longer pass through stars, sprites, or border. Applied to both player masers and Jovian beams.

summary: Beam erase plots black instead of restoring saved pixels
description: Replaced all beam-restore-slice calls with 0 beam-draw-slice. Eliminates stale pixel artifacts when sprites move after beam trace. Sprite refresh cycle redraws stars and sprites naturally.

summary: Full tactical view redraw after kills
description: refresh-after-kill now clears tactical area (rows 0-143, 4608 bytes) and redraws border, stars, storm stars, event horizon, base, living Jovians, and ship. Status panel preserved. Eliminates all post-kill artifacts.

summary: Scanner degradation when pdmg-scan is low
description: Below 40% scanner health, random cells show @ instead of real data. Probability scales with damage — at 0% the entire map is garbled. Superseded by #310 implementation.

summary: Deflector cap when pdmg-defl is low
description: Maximum shield setting should be capped at pdmg-defl percentage. e.g. deflectors at 60% means max shield setting is 60. Depends on #71 (deflector command implementation).

summary: Kernel: TYPE primitive ( addr len -- )
description: Add TYPE to kernel — loops over len bytes at addr calling EMIT for each. ~20 bytes at $E000. Enables string-based text output, saving app space vs char-by-char EMIT calls.

summary: Kernel: COUNT primitive ( c-addr -- addr+1 len )
description: Add COUNT to kernel — converts counted string (length byte + chars) to addr+len form. ~12 bytes at $E000.

summary: fc.py: S" and ." string literal support
description: Add S" ... " syntax to fc.py. Compiles inline string data in thread with BRANCH to skip over it, pushes addr+len at runtime. Add ." as shorthand for S" ... " TYPE. New slit IR node. Breakeven at 4+ chars vs CHAR-by-CHAR.

summary: Library: rg-type ( addr len -- ) for RG6 text output
description: Add rg-type to rg-text.fs — Forth word that loops over string calling rg-emit for each byte. Keeps RG6-specific code out of kernel. Enables S" ... " rg-type pattern.

summary: Space Warp: replace CHAR chains with string literals
description: Replace ~75 CHAR X rg-emit chains in spacewarp.fs with S" ..." rg-type calls. Estimated savings ~459 bytes of app space.

summary: Jovian AI: FLEE state (retreat when health is low)
description: Health below 50% triggers flee: JOV-STATE=2, moves away from player, stops firing, emotion set to fear (blue sprite). jov-flee CODE word.

summary: Jovian AI: IDLE state (opportunistic firing)
description: IDLE state: Jovian does not chase player but fires if player is within line of sight. Implemented in #204. Uses JOV-STATE byte.

summary: Jovian AI: base targeting priority
description: Jovians target bases when present. Jovian 0 always attacks base; wounded Jovians (DMG<50) flee to base. Base-targeting Jovians stop at 30px and attack from range. After 180 frames (~3 sec) of sustained attack, base is destroyed: explosion, galaxy bit cleared, panel updated, loss check triggered. Split targeting, attack timer, and full destruction sequence implemented.

summary: Jovian AI: Jovian-to-Jovian collision avoidance
description: Implemented as part of #214 — jov-blocked? checks all other living Jovians at 8px threshold.

summary: Jovian AI: inter-quadrant movement
description: Jovians should move between galaxy quadrants over time, especially toward quadrants containing bases. When a Jovian leaves a quadrant, decrement that quadrant's Jovian count and increment the destination's. Drives the strategic layer — player must prioritize which quadrants to defend.

summary: Add version number to title screen
description: Display V0.9 in lower-right of title screen. Row 22 was off-screen (RG6 has rows 0-18), corrupting app code at $2180. Fixed to row 18. Also shifted game data region by $20 ($7620→$7640 through $77A1→$77C1) because the version string code grew the binary past the old GALAXY address.

summary: Move font data to kernel as raw FCB bytes
description: Moved 59 glyphs (472 bytes) from font-art.fs Forth literals into kernel.asm as DOVAR + FCB data. init-font becomes: font-data $9000 472 CMOVE. Eliminated fp variable, fb, f8, f0 helper words. Saved 1,538 bytes of app space (21529 -> 19991). Kernel cost: 474 bytes. Font loads in <1ms vs visible pause before.

summary: Move sprite data to kernel as raw FCB bytes
description: init-sprites in spacewarp.fs costs ~394 bytes of app space to push-and-store 60 bytes of sprite pixel data via Forth literals. Moved 5 sprites into kernel.asm as DOVAR + FCB data (62 bytes kernel). init-sprites becomes: sprite-data SPR-SHIP 60 CMOVE. Saved 380 bytes of app space (21909 -> 21529). Kernel cost: 62 bytes from 6.6K headroom.

summary: Add PROX-SCAN kernel primitive for spatial proximity queries
description: Generic kernel primitive: prox-scan ( cx cy radius array count -- bitmask ). Scans (x,y) byte pairs, returns bitmask of entries within Manhattan distance. ~70 bytes kernel code. Uses return stack for loop params, handles 8-bit overflow on distance sum.

summary: Refactor proximity-damage to use PROX-SCAN primitive
description: Replaced distance calculation loop with prox-scan call. Eliminated pd-i variable. Damage application uses bitmask from prox-scan, ship check stays inline. Saved 46 bytes app (19991 -> 19945). Main benefit is speed: inner loop runs in assembly instead of ~300 ITC cycles per Jovian.

summary: CODE word: tick-jovians-inner (tick loop + threshold)
description: Convert tick-jovians DO/LOOP to CODE. Inlines threshold calc from genome bytes (~25cy vs 490cy Forth). Returns 3-bit mask of which Jovians need think/flee. Forth wrapper dispatches. Savings: ~4,140cy to ~200cy for 3 non-thinking Jovians.

summary: CODE word: jov-pull
description: Convert jov-pull to inline 6809 assembly CODE word. Inner loop of jov-gravity — >R/R@ cached byte-pair comparison pattern repeated 4 times for X/Y pull toward target. Called 0-8 times/frame. Estimated 3-4x speedup.

summary: CODE word: star-pull
description: Convert star-pull to inline 6809 assembly CODE word. Same pattern as jov-pull but for ship — four conditional byte increments on SHIP-POS vs sg-sx/sg-sy. Called 0-4 times/frame. Estimated 3-4x speedup.

summary: CODE word: check-collisions
description: Convert check-collisions to inline 6809 assembly CODE word. Loop over stars with repeated C@ - abs 3 < pattern on byte pairs, plus black hole check. Called every frame. Estimated 2.5x speedup.

summary: CODE word: gravity-well
description: Convert gravity-well to inline 6809 assembly CODE word. Tiered distance calculation (30/20/10/6px thresholds) + 4 conditional pulls on ship position. Nested IF chain maps to branch table. Called every frame when black hole present. Estimated 2-2.5x speedup.

summary: CODE word: tick-dock
description: Convert tick-dock to inline 6809 assembly CODE word. 5 identical damage repair patterns (pdmg-ion through pdmg-masr) plus energy recharge with bit-masking tiers. Lots of redundant @/! sequences. Called every frame when docked. Estimated 2.5-3x speedup.

summary: Jovian genome data structure + gen-genome CODE word
description: Allocate genome RAM (12 bytes at $77C5), intent output (9 bytes at $77D1), sprite workspace (12 bytes at $77DA). Write gen-genome CODE word for difficulty-biased random generation at spawn. Integrate with existing Jovian spawn logic. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 1.

summary: jov-think CODE word — data-driven AI replacement
description: Replace Forth AI words (jov-targets-base?, move-one-jovian, jov-blocked?, parts of tick-jovians) with jov-think CODE word. Read 4 genome bytes, extract traits via shifts/masks, compute intent (dx, dy, fire-flag) via lookup tables. ~60-80 bytes of 6809. Must produce identical visible behavior initially. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 1.

summary: Tick frequency variation — per-Jovian tick thresholds
description: Each Jovian gets tick threshold from speed_modifier + pilot_skill genome bits. Fast aces tick every 2 frames, slow dolts every 8. Verify smooth animation at all rates. Confirm equal or better performance than current system. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 2.

summary: Emotion system — jov-emotion-tick + event hooks
description: Write jov-emotion-tick CODE word (decay toward genome baseline + stimulus application). Add event hooks: player enters quadrant (+2 alarm), player fires (±2 rage/fear), fellow killed (+3 extreme), damages player (+1 confidence), time decay, docking boldness, detection alertness, hazard fear. Emotion modifies fire rate, speed, avoidance, engagement range, flee threshold. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 3.

summary: Detection & awareness — jov-detect CODE word
description: Write jov-detect CODE word: pilot_skill + emotion_level vs Manhattan distance. Failed detection = idle/patrol. Firing always reveals player to all Jovians. High-mood quadrants get detection bonus. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 4.

summary: Quadrant mood persistence — mood grid save/restore
description: Allocate 64-byte mood grid at $7E00. Save aggregated Jovian emotions on quadrant exit, seed new Jovian emotions on entry. Decay by 1 per stardate toward neutral between visits. Unvisited sectors drift aggressive. After killing all Jovians, high mood persists for replacements. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 5.

summary: Algorithmic sprite generation — procedural Jovian sprites
description: Procedural per-Jovian sprites from genome appearance seed. Per-row PRNG bit pattern with half-template mirror (space invader style). Emotion color tinting: fear=blue, neutral=white, rage=red. Regenerates on band threshold crossing. Origin nibble set from sector hash for future density tuning.

summary: Regional character — origin hash + genome bias
description: Use origin nibble (sector hash from quadrant coordinates) to bias genome generation regionally. Jovians from same galactic region share tendencies. Final aesthetic tuning pass. Gate debug overlay behind key for release. See AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md Phase 7.

summary: Star gravity too weak — no noticeable pull effect
description: Widened Jovian star gravity range from 8px to 12px with two-tier pull rate: close (3-6px) every 2 frames, far (6-12px) every 4 frames.

summary: Emotion system edge cases — false docking, warp state
description: Edge cases in emotion event hooks need investigation. Observed: possible false docking state after emotion hooks fire, warp command not responding in some situations. May be related to emotion-all firing during state transitions (quadrant entry, docking, warping). Need to test: emotion hooks during warp, emotion hooks when 0 Jovians alive, fire-maser emotion hook with no living targets, do-dock hook interaction with docked state checks.

summary: Gravity slingshot mechanics for ships and missiles
description: Orbital mechanics around stars and black holes: objects near gravity wells could orbit rather than just get pulled in. Jovians in idle state could orbit a nearby star. Missiles passing through a gravity well could curve. Ships could use gravity assist for speed boost. Requires vector-based movement model for affected objects. Low priority — aesthetic/gameplay polish for later.

summary: Jovian missile avoidance — dodge active missiles
description: Aware Jovians should treat an active missile as a dynamic obstacle and bias movement away from it. Check missile position each tick, if within avoidance radius (~15px?), add evasion vector to intent. Only aware Jovians react. Depends on #176 (detection/awareness). Separate from static obstacle avoidance (stars/black holes/bases).

summary: Flee behavior — peaceful/fearful Jovians move away from player
description: Already implemented via emotion-driven engagement range in jov-think CODE word (phases 3-5). Fearful Jovians (emotion 0) maintain 65px distance and retreat if player closes within 62px. Health-based flee (DMG<50) handled by jov-flee CODE word + state 2. Issue predated the emotion system implementation.

summary: Jovian quadrant flee — wounded/terrified Jovians exit to adjacent quadrant
description: When a Jovian is badly wounded (DMG<25) or deeply fearful (emotion<3), it should move toward the nearest screen edge and exit the quadrant. On exit: erase sprite, decrement qjovians, increment Jovian count in an adjacent galaxy quadrant (direction of flee). Requires: edge-seeking behavior in jov-think when flee conditions met, boundary detection for exit, galaxy array update. The fleeing Jovian retains its damage state in the destination quadrant.

summary: Jovian reinforcements — adjacent Jovians warp into active quadrant
description: When a fellow Jovian is attacked (maser hit, kill), Jovians in adjacent quadrants may respond by warping in as reinforcements. Chance scales with quadrant mood and number of adjacent Jovians. On entry: decrement source quadrant count, increment qjovians (if <3), spawn at screen edge with high aggression emotion. Creates a living galaxy where fights attract attention. Limit to 1 reinforcement per event to avoid flooding. Depends on #177 (mood persistence) for mood-based triggering.

summary: Jovian galactic migration — aggressive Jovians drift toward hot zones
description: Between stardates, aggressive Jovians in non-adjacent quadrants gradually migrate toward high-mood sectors (where fighting happened). Rate: 1 migration per stardate, only high-aggression Jovians. Creates strategic depth: sustained fighting in one area attracts the fleet. Player counters by warping to distant quadrants to spread out encounters. Implementation: on stardate tick, pick a random high-aggression Jovian from a calm quadrant, move to an adjacent quadrant closer to the highest-mood sector. Depends on #177 (mood persistence).

summary: Sound system — blocked by all-RAM mode
description: DAC sound at $FF20 works before all-RAM mode ($FFDF) but not after. Extensive investigation (2026-03-26): confirmed DAC writes produce audio from BASIC and from bootstrap before ORCC/FFDF. After all-RAM mode, DAC writes are silent even with mode-switch back to ROM ($FFDE), IRQ unmasking, PIA1 reinit, and temp stack relocation. The return stack at $DFxx is in ROM overlay range ($8000-$FEFF) which prevents naive mode switching. A working SND_PLAY subroutine was built at $0E19 (bootstrap area) with S-register save/restore — it runs and returns correctly but produces no audio. Root cause unknown — may be XRoar emulation behavior or a PIA state dependency not yet identified. Needs: Lomont hardware doc or XRoar source analysis. Sound demo at src/sound/ with working library at forth/lib/sound.fs for future development.

summary: Visual tuning harness for procedural Jovian sprites
description: Complete toolchain: sprtest.fs (CoCo test app, all 256 seeds), gen_sprite_report.py (SVG catalog with scatter/grid/page views), sprite_rater.py (mobile hot-or-not web app), sprite_data.py (seed descriptions and ratings). All 256 seeds captured in 3 colors. Next: use rater to collect human ratings, then map seeds to Jovian disposition in the game.

summary: Numeric keypad Enter key not recognized as Return
description: Fixed via XRoar -kbd-bind KP_Enter=pre:enter in Makefile. KP_Enter (sym 0xff8d) was not being translated to CoCo Enter by -kbd-translate. The pre: prefix preempts the translation layer and maps directly to the CoCo key.

summary: CODE optimization tier 1 — merge sprite gen, convert jov-emotion!
description: Merge gen-jov-sprite Forth wrapper + jov-sprite-gen CODE engine + emo-color into a single gen-jov-sprite CODE word. Convert jov-emotion! from Forth to CODE. Saves 184 bytes.

summary: CODE optimization tier 2 — kill, draw-live, regen, color
description: Convert 4 words to CODE: jov-kill, draw-jovians-live, jov-check-regen, emo-color+jov-color-band. Estimated savings: ~216 bytes.

summary: CODE optimization tier 3 — emotion helpers and min
description: Convert small Forth words to CODE: jov-emotion-decay, jov-emotion-base, jov-emotion@, min. Estimated savings: ~68 bytes.

summary: Jovian beam targets ship instead of base during base attack
description: fire-jbeam always computes direction toward SHIP-POS. When a Jovian is attacking the base (tick-base-attack), the beam visually targets the player ship while the base takes damage from a hidden timer. Need fire-jbeam-at-base variant that aims at BASE-POS instead of SHIP-POS, called when the Jovian is in base-attack mode.

summary: Support 2 simultaneous missiles in flight
description: Convert missile state from scalar variables to 2-element arrays. fire-missile allocates first free slot, tick-missile loops both, sprite cycle erases/draws both. Estimated ~150 bytes code + 36 bytes RAM.

summary: Relocate game data structures from $75xx-$7Exx to $8000+
description: Move all game data CONSTANTs (GALAXY, object positions, sprite buffers, beam paths, explosion buffer, mood grid, Jovian AI state/genome, pixel lookup tables) from $75A4-$7E3F to $8000+. Update all CONSTANT definitions in spacewarp.fs. Add memory clear of $8000+ region at game init. This frees ~3,600 bytes between code end and $8000 for code growth. Headroom jumps from ~9 bytes to ~2,600+ bytes.

summary: Update hardcoded addresses in CODE words after data relocation
description: After #196, update all hardcoded memory addresses in CODE words that reference game data. Affected: jov-sprite-gen ($75F8 JOV-SPR0, $77C5 JOV-GENOME, $77C8 JOV-GENOME+3, $77DA JOV-SPRWORK, $77DC-$77E5 scratch), jov-spr-xy ($75F8, $768A JOV-POS), jov-bg-xy ($75A4 JOV-BG0, $75F8, $768A), jov-bg-old-xy ($75A4, $75F8, $77BE JOV-OLDX, $77C1 JOV-OLDY), save-jov-oldpos-n ($768A, $77BE), jov-flee ($7694 SHIP-POS, $7695), jov-emotion! ($77C8), jov-emo@ ($77C8), jov-emotion-base ($77C5). Each CODE word needs its literal addresses updated to match the new $8000+ layout.

summary: Verify fc.py VARIABLE allocation doesn't conflict with relocated data
description: After #196-#197, verify that fc.py-allocated VARIABLEs (which remain in the $2000+ code stream) don't overlap with any game data at the new $8000+ addresses. Ensure init-sprites and other initialization code correctly references the new sprite data locations. Run full gameplay test: warp, combat, explosions, docking, SOS alerts, flee behavior.

summary: Resize missile sprites from 5x5 to 3x3 and fix animation rate
description: Shrink missile sprites from 5x5 to 3x3 in kernel sprite-data. Update SPR-MSL2 address (offset changes from 48 to 41), init-sprites copy count (60→46), and all missile centering offsets (2→1). Fix missile animation rate from every-frame to every-2-frames for visible alternation.

summary: End-game rating based on stardate vs level
description: On win screen, show a rating based on stardates elapsed vs difficulty level. Lower stardates = better rating. Simple tier: e.g. stardates/level ratio maps to Ensign/Captain/Commander/Admiral. Replaces issue #80 (scoring) with a lighter approach.

summary: Tiered main loop scheduling + split sprite cycle
description: Split sprite cycle: ship-only cycle when just player moves (skip Jovian bg ops), full cycle only when jov-moved. Schedule slow game logic at lower rates. Remove duplicate game logic block. Target: solid 60fps for common case (player moving, Jovians idle).

summary: Remove tactical border frame, keep bottom separator
description: Remove top/left/right border lines from draw-border, keeping only the bottom separator at y=143. Expand sprite/beam/spawn bounds from min 4 to min 1 to use freed edge space.

summary: Increase dock energy recharge rate 4x
description: Quadruple the docking energy recharge curve. 0-19%: +4/frame, 20-39%: +2/frame, 40-59%: +1/frame, 60-79%: +1/2frames, 80-99%: +1/4frames.

summary: IDLE Jovians fire opportunistically when player is in range
description: Expand pick-jovian to select IDLE (state 0) Jovians for firing when the player is within their detection range (jov-detect?). IDLE Jovians that fire do not transition to ATTACK — they remain IDLE. Reuses existing detection infrastructure (pilot skill + emotion).

summary: Latch keyboard input to prevent missed key presses
description: Add latch-key word that polls KEY? and saves nonzero results into key-latch variable. Call latch-key at multiple points in the game loop (after AI/physics, after beam drawing) so brief taps are captured even when frame processing is slow. process-key consumes and clears the latch.

summary: Self-destruct countdown not displaying
description: exec-command calls draw-cmd-prompt after do-destruct, overwriting the countdown digit with COMMAND. Also tick-destruct was in the every-8th-frame block, making the 30-frame timer take 8x longer. Fix: skip draw-cmd-prompt when sd-active, move tick-destruct to every-frame.

summary: Death screen: clear COND, show DESTROYED before explosion, no ship reappear
description: Clear row 17 before writing death status so COND text doesn't linger. Show DESTROYED before explosion animation in all death paths. After self-destruct explosion, redraw tactical without ship so it doesn't flash back.

summary: CLEAR key cancels pending command input
description: CLEAR key ($0C, mapped to backslash in XRoar) cancels current command input and restores COMMAND prompt. Resets cmd-state to 0 and redraws prompt.

summary: Emotion-driven engagement distance in jov-think
description: Aggressive Jovians close in on the player, fearful ones keep distance. Range = 20 + (15-emotion)*3: rage=20px, fear=65px. Dead zone +-3px prevents oscillation. Jovians retreat if too close, hold in dead zone, approach if too far. Modifies jov-think CODE word.

summary: Remove dead code and deduplicate beam clamping
description: Deleted unused variables (rp-tmp, gi, grav-pull), unused words (q-pack, erase-ship). Added max CODE word. Refactored clamp-beam/clamp-jbeam to use min/max. 256 bytes recovered.

summary: Add MDIST kernel primitive and refactor call sites
description: MDIST ( addr1 addr2 -- d ) Manhattan distance between two (x,y) byte pairs. 7 call sites converted, 262 bytes saved. Remaining abs uses are bounding box checks or loose values.

summary: Fleeing Jovians avoid stars via apply-intent
description: jov-flee now writes to JOV-INTENT instead of directly to JOV-POS. apply-intent handles obstacle avoidance (stars, black holes, base). Star avoidance distance scales with pilot skill (6-13px). Star gravity gated by avoidance distance so skilled pilots resist pull.

summary: Handedness-based obstacle avoidance direction
description: When apply-intent finds the direct path blocked, use genome handedness (byte 1 bits 3-2) to choose which axis to try first. Now implemented in apply-intent CODE word (#241) — 42 bytes of assembly, no jitter. Handedness >= 2 (right/strong) tries Y-axis slide first; 0-1 (neutral/left) tries X-axis first. Gives Jovians personality when navigating around obstacles.

summary: Sprite collision — everything bumps
description: Three cases: (1) Endeavour blocked by Jovians — ship-jov-blocked? in move-ship. (2) Jovians blocked by Endeavour — SHIP-POS check in jov-blocked?. (3) Jovians blocked by each other — other-Jovian proximity in jov-blocked?. Also fixed beam-trace buffer overflow (200px cap), jov-think genome index bug (S+4 not S+3), and edge clamping (4-123 x 4-136).

summary: Merge jov-blocked? and ship-jov-blocked? into single CODE word
description: jov-blocked? converted to CODE word (488cy base + 77cy/star + 116cy/Jovian = 1,105cy; was 13,800cy Forth). Signature ( x y i -- flag ). QCOUNTS shadow bytes at $80B0. ship-jov-blocked? subsumed into move-ship CODE (#244). hc-jx, hc-jy, jblk, jov-dist removed. jov-blocked? itself later inlined into apply-intent CODE (#241).

summary: Refactor move-ship and frame-smooth game loop
description: 1) try-move helper: 4 copy-pasted direction blocks → 14 lines, saved 254 bytes. 2) cancel-msl helper: 3 identical patterns → 1 word, saved 42 bytes (fixed recursive definition bug from replace_all). 3) tick-jovians: limit 1 AI think per frame (was up to 3). 4) jov-gravity: round-robin 1 Jovian per frame (was all 3). 5) Background tasks: split 7 tasks into 2 groups on frames 0 and 4 (was all on frame 0). Net size: 24552 → 24360, saved 192 bytes, headroom 216.

summary: Non-blocking damage report and long range scan
description: Damage report (cmd 1) and long range scan (cmd 3) no longer block the game loop with KEY DROP. New overlay variable gates rendering — game loop keeps running (VSYNC, key processing) while overlay is displayed. Any keypress dismisses and redraws tactical view. Damage report refreshes live every 16 frames. Cost: +132 bytes.

summary: Missile ghost ship artifact on deactivation
description: Missile bg-save captured ship pixels at launch. On deactivation (OOB, hit, cancel), restore-msl-bg stamped ghost ship at missile death position. Fix: msl-kill uses spr-erase-box + jov-moved flag for clean full redraw instead of restoring stale bg buffer.

summary: Ship ghost artifact after Jovian explosion
description: save-ship-bg captured Jovian sprite pixels when ship overlapped a Jovian. After kill, restore-ship-bg stamped ghost at old position. Fix: reorder render pipeline — save-ship-bg before draw-jovians-live so bg buffer only contains stars/background. Also sync save-ship-pos in refresh-after-kill and clear moved/jov-moved flags to prevent double-draw.

summary: Objects spawn too close to viewport edge
description: rnd-x returned 1-123, rnd-y returned 1-139. Ship sprite extends 3px from center, so positions near edge caused sprite wrap into border area. Fix: rnd-x now returns 5-120, rnd-y returns 5-134. Also saved 36 bytes from simpler arithmetic vs DUP/clamp pattern.

summary: COND not updating after Jovian kill
description: update-cond only ran on background task slot (every 8 frames). After missile kill, COND stayed YELLOW instead of GREEN because reinforcement spawned before next update-cond. Fix: call update-cond immediately after kill chain (jov-kill, apply-beam-hit).

summary: Overlay dismiss leaves ship ghost
description: dismiss-overlay used draw-quadrant + separate save calls with wrong order (bg saved after sprites drawn). Fix: dismiss-overlay now calls refresh-after-kill which uses correct save-before-draw order.

summary: Combat freeze with active beam after kill
description: refresh-after-kill cleared VRAM but did not cancel active beams/missiles. Stale beam path data caused render loop corruption. Fix: refresh-after-kill now calls cancel-beam cancel-jbeam cancel-msl before clearing.

summary: Gravity loops dominate frame budget
description: star-gravity (5 stars × mdist) and jov-gravity (3 Jovians × 5 stars + bhole) caused visible jitter. Confirmed via selective disable testing. Fix: gate all gravity distance loops on grav-tick 3 AND — skip 75% of frames. Contact-range checks (<6px bhole, <3px star) still run every frame. Simplified gravity-well tiered pull to single 1px pull. Saved 46 bytes. Frame budget rebalanced: even frames (physics+AI), odd frames (gravity+BG tasks).

summary: Frame drop detector (temporary)
description: Added vsync-check word that tests VSYNC flag before waiting — if already set, frame was dropped. Displayed D0/D1/etc on panel. Result: 0 drops observed in all scenarios. Confirmed frame budget is solid (peak 45% utilization). Removed after testing.

summary: Add passive energy regeneration
description: Ship reactor should slowly regenerate energy over time (e.g. +1 every 16-32 frames). Not enough to sustain combat indefinitely, but enough that breaking contact allows recovery. Docking should remain much faster.

summary: Add shield energy drain over time
description: Superseded by shield redesign (#292-#297). Shields no longer passively drain — cost is paid upfront on activation, and shields degrade from incoming damage.

summary: Decouple shields from outgoing maser damage
description: Currently shields reduce the Endever's own maser damage using the same formula as incoming damage reduction. Remove this penalty — let masers fire at full power regardless of shield setting. The energy drain from shields (issue #227) provides the trade-off instead.

summary: Prevent weapons firing with insufficient energy
description: Currently weapons fire even at 0 energy (cost clamps to 0). Add a minimum-energy check: don't fire maser if penergy < MASER-COST (10), don't fire missile if penergy < MSL-COST (5). Prevents accidental self-drain to death.

summary: Scanner degradation gameplay effect
description: Implemented via #310 — scanner below 40% garbles LRS cells with @. Superseded.

summary: Status/damage report redraws every frame (flicker)
description: The status/damage report screen redraws continuously every frame, causing visible flicker. Should draw once and only update values that change.

summary: Game simulation stops during status/damage report
description: While viewing the status/damage report, game simulation appears to pause — Jovians don't move, fire, or migrate. Game logic should continue ticking during the report view, making it a tactical decision to check status mid-battle.

summary: Starbase can spawn in gravity well of star or black hole
description: Starbases sometimes spawn too close to a star or black hole, making docking difficult or impossible due to gravity pulling the ship away. Fixed: unified pos-safe? word, black hole placed before base, all entities use safe-place. See #342.

summary: Show command name instead of number in command prompt
description: When a command number is pressed, replace COMMAND with the command name (WARP, SHIELDS, MASERS, MISSILE, DESTRUCT) instead of just the digit. More intuitive than '6?180'.

summary: WARP auto-executes after 2nd coordinate digit
description: Warp command should execute immediately after the player types the second coordinate digit. No return key needed. E.g., typing 6 then 3 then 5 warps to (3,5) instantly.

summary: Self-destruct auto-executes after final PIN digit
description: Self-destruct should engage (or cancel) immediately after the final digit of the PIN code (3) is typed. No return key needed.

summary: Show feedback when command is rejected
description: When a command fails (insufficient energy, no missiles, etc.), briefly flash a reason like NO ENERGY or NO MISSILES instead of silently doing nothing.

summary: High difficulty levels cause frame drops and sluggish movement
description: Umbrella issue. After #215 (jov-blocked? CODE) and #245 (selective inline), think cost dropped 17K→5.6Kcy but 29/60 frames still over budget. Remaining bottlenecks: every-frame base (4,436cy), full render (7,732cy), gravity odd (5,000cy), post-render (2,046cy). See FRAME_BUDGET.md for measured breakdown.

summary: CODE word: jov-blocked? (critical bottleneck)
description: DONE — duplicate of #215. jov-blocked? converted to CODE word: 488cy base + 77cy/star + 116cy/Jovian = 1,105cy (was 13,800cy Forth). jov-dist and jov-avoid-dist inlined. hc-jx, hc-jy, jblk variables removed.

summary: CODE word: apply-intent (combine with jov-blocked?)
description: apply-intent converted to CODE word with inlined jov-blocked? obstacle check. 3-tier fallback (both axes, x-only, y-only) runs in 6809 assembly. jov-blocked? Forth word removed (replaced by dummy). jtk-nx, jtk-ny variables removed. Cost: 752cy base + 617cy per @chk call (5 stars + 2 Jovians). Think total: jov-think(689) + apply-intent(1,369 typical) = 2,058cy (was 17,000cy original, 8.3x speedup).

summary: CODE word: ship-gravity (merged gravity-well + star-gravity)
description: Merged gravity-well and star-gravity into single ship-gravity CODE word. Inlines pull subroutine with step parameter (2px for close bhole, 1px for mid/stars). Eliminated xyn-pull, xy-pull, star-pull Forth words. 5,776cy to 476cy.

summary: Refactor jov-gravity: split contact kills from pull
description: Split jov-gravity into jov-contact CODE (300cy every frame, checks mdist<3 to stars/bhole) + jov-gravity-pull CODE (400cy on qualifying odd frames). Contact check now runs every frame (was every 4th odd frame). Eliminated jov-gravity-one, jov-gravity, jov-pull.

summary: CODE word: move-ship (every-frame keyboard + movement)
description: move-ship converted to CODE word. Inlines 4x PIA keyboard scan, try-move bounds/collision, ship-on-base?, ship-jov-blocked?. Removed Forth words: try-move, ship-on-base?, ship-jov-blocked?, ship-dx, ship-dy. Cost: 669cy base + 108cy/Jovian = 993cy idle (was 1,633cy Forth). Also added FVAR_* EQU export to fc.py (two-pass assembly with dummy then real variable addresses).

summary: fc.py: inline CONSTANT values as LIT (selective)
description: fc.py inline_constants() inlines CONSTANT references as LIT values. Initially selective (<=3 refs, MAX_INLINE_REFS=3, saved 262 bytes). Then raised to inline all constants (#246). 23 low-ref + 22 high-ref constants inlined. Each ref: 80cy->31cy (49cy saved). App savings from selective phase: 262 bytes.

summary: fc.py: inline high-ref CONSTANTs when space available
description: MAX_INLINE_REFS raised to 999 (inline all). High-ref constants inlined: SHIP-POS(44 refs), JOV-POS(43), JOV-DMG(38), BASE-POS(16), BHOLE-POS(16), etc. Cost +324 bytes but saves ~49cy per reference across all paths. Combined with #245, all CONSTANT definitions eliminated from output.

summary: Deduplicate latch-key (called 3x per frame)
description: latch-key (353cy) was called 3 times per frame: inside process-key, standalone pre-VSYNC, and post-VSYNC. Removed 2 redundant calls. Saves 706cy on every frame (42,360cy/sec). App: 24,261 → 24,257 bytes.

summary: CODE word: save/restore-jov-bgs
description: Converted save-jov-bgs and restore-jov-bgs from Forth wrappers to CODE words with inline loop, alive check, sprite centering, underflow clamps (BCS/CLRB), and 4x7 VRAM copy. Previous attempt failed due to missing underflow clamps (same root cause as #253). Savings: ~1,200cy/frame from eliminating ITC dispatch overhead. Binary: 24,268 bytes (+155 from Forth version), headroom 308 bytes.

summary: draw-stars CODE with fixed colors
description: Converted draw-stars to CODE word inlining rg-pset loop. Fixed colors per star based on position hash ((x+y)&2+1). 1,840cy to ~400cy.

summary: Fix apply-intent CMPB off-by-one (Jovians frozen since #241)
description: apply-intent CODE @chk subroutine used CMPB 10,S for self-skip check but i was at S+9 (LBSR pushes 2 bytes, not 3). Jovians always found themselves as blocking obstacle (distance 0 < 8). Fixed: CMPB 9,S.

summary: Fix sprite coordinate underflow near screen edges
description: jov-spr-xy, jov-bg-xy, jov-bg-old-xy computed pos-width/2 and pos-height/2 without clamping. Near top/left edges, result wrapped to 250+ causing VRAM addresses in code space (+), corrupting the program. Fixed: BCS/CLRB clamp after NEGB+ADDB. Latent bug hidden while Jovians were frozen.

summary: Fix two STA ,-S illegal addressing modes
description: Pre-decrement by 1 (,-S) is illegal for store operations on 6809. STA ,-S in apply-intent @chk and move-ship @spd silently produced wrong behavior. Fixed: replaced with PSHS A.

summary: Sudden catastrophic VRAM corruption during gameplay
description: jov-bg-xy CODE word was missing BCS/CLRB underflow clamps on both X and Y coordinates. When a Jovian moved near the top-left edge, pos - width/2 wrapped to 250+, causing bg-save-7 to write 28 bytes far outside VRAM. Fix: added BCS/CLRB after NEGB+ADDB for both axes, matching the pattern already in jov-spr-xy and jov-bg-old-xy (commit d018665).

summary: Slot-based think scheduling + collisions to odd frames
