description: Replaced dynamic tick/threshold system with deterministic time slots. think-slot rotates 0-1-2 each even frame, genome skip factor (1-6) controls frequency. Moved check-collisions to odd frames. All even frames under budget at level 10. Frames over budget: 30/60 -> 8/60, peak 17,160 -> 15,790cy.

summary: Jitter when Jovians fire on the Endeavour
description: Visible jitter/stutter when Jovians are about to fire their beam at the player ship. Likely caused by jbeam firing logic (fire-jbeam, tick-jbeam) spiking cycle cost on the frame it triggers. Needs profiling of the jbeam fire path.

summary: BG slot 1 redistribution to non-gravity frames
description: Moved BG slot 1 from N AND 7 = 1 to N AND 7 = 3, shifting 1,360cy of background tasks off the gravity odd frames. Combined with check-collisions move to odd frames, all 60 frames now under budget at level 10.

summary: Split fire-jbeam into trace + resolve across 2 frames
description: Split the ~15,700cy jbeam fire spike into fire-jbeam-trace (frame A: pick-jovian + cancel + trace + cooldown) and fire-jbeam-resolve (frame B: find-obstacle + ship-hit + start animation). tick-jbeam uses deferred path; tick-base-attack still uses synchronous fire-jbeam.

summary: Convert pick-jovian, jbeam-ship-hit?, clamp-jbeam to CODE
description: pick-jovian: 4,256->315cy (inlines jov-detect? logic). jbeam-ship-hit?: 2,126->145cy (pixel bbox loop). clamp-jbeam: 1,177->165cy (4 min/max chains). Total fire path savings ~7,000cy. Binary 80 bytes smaller.

summary: Red artifacts from jbeam erase when ship moves
description: Beam erase now paints black + redraws stars (~400cy) instead of beam-restore-slice. Avoids overlapping-buffer conflicts when both maser and jbeam are active simultaneously. beam-scrub-sprites zeros saved_color under ship and all living Jovians at trace time (after obstacle detection) so beam-find-obstacle still truncates correctly at sprites. Fixes: beam going through ship, stale sprite pixels on restore, dual-beam overlap artifacts.

summary: Maser firing causes frame timing pause
description: Player maser fire path (fire-maser) causes visible stutter. Likely same issue as jbeam — beam-trace + beam-find-obstacle + beam-check-path-hits all run on one frame. May need similar 2-frame split or CODE conversion.

summary: Convert beam-check-path-hits, beam-find-obstacle, clamp-beam to CODE
description: beam-check-path-hits: 4,204->250cy (nested pixel x Jovian bbox loop). beam-find-obstacle: 1,239->100cy (pixel scan for obstacles, shared by maser+jbeam). clamp-beam: 1,177->165cy. fire-maser total: 26,597->20,492cy. fire-jbeam-resolve: 1,947->808cy (via shared beam-find-obstacle). Binary 24,039 bytes.

summary: Add HSYNC beam-chasing to Space Warp sprite rendering
description: Added HSYNC beam-chasing to Space Warp. After VSYNC, a max-draw-y CODE word computes the bottom-most sprite row across ship (old+new) and all living Jovians (pos+old), then wait-past-row delays until the CRT beam has passed that row before any VRAM writes. Blanking offset: 70 lines (NTSC). Cost: ~20cy per scanline waited. Prerequisite #248 (bg-jov CODE) freed ~1,200cy to absorb the wait cost. Binary: 24,372 bytes, headroom 204 bytes.

summary: save/restore-jov-bgs CODE used wrong address for JOV-DMG
description: The #248 CODE conversion of save-jov-bgs and restore-jov-bgs used $8050 (BASE-POS) instead of $8056 (JOV-DMG) for the alive check. This caused incorrect bg save/restore — reading base X/Y coordinates as alive/dead flags — producing ship sprite trails during gameplay. Fixed both CODE words to use $8056.

summary: Bounce demo: HSYNC beam-chasing testbed with 4 balls
description: New demo at src/bounce/ — 4 bouncing balls in RG6 artifact-color mode with three rendering modes (0=free-running, 1=VSYNC only, 2=VSYNC+HSYNC beam-chasing). Time-sliced rendering (2 balls/frame, 30fps per ball). Font HUD shows blanking offset and mode. Up/down arrows adjust offset, 0/1/2 keys switch modes. Proved HSYNC beam-chasing technique before applying to Space Warp (#262). Includes README documenting findings and CoCo keyboard matrix reference.

summary: Kernel: vsync CODE word + wait-past-row blanking offset fix
description: Converted vsync from Forth (BEGIN/UNTIL polling loop) to CODE word for tighter detection. Fixed wait-past-row blanking offset from 35 to 70 HSYNC lines — 70 matches the actual NTSC vertical blanking interval (262 total lines - 192 visible). Both in forth/lib/screen.fs.

summary: Update docs/reference.html: fix keyboard matrix, add missing libraries
description: reference.html has the old wrong keyboard matrix (all arrows in C3). Fix to match corrected keyboard.fs and kernel KEY_TABLE. Add missing library sections: rg-pixel.fs, sprite.fs, beam.fs, trig.fs, sound.fs, dac.fs, screen.fs (wait-past-row, count-blanking). Update memory map for all-RAM configuration.

summary: Add cross-references and headers to markdown working notes
description: Add research/exploratory header to COCO_INTERRUPTS.md. Link USERGUIDE.md and MARKETING.md from spacewarp/README.md. Cross-reference SPEC.md and AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md. Trim out-of-scope hardware spec from COCO_RENOVATION.md.

summary: Deduplicate repeated string literals
description: Factor repeated S" strings into named words to save space. DESTROYED x6 (~49 net bytes), AGAIN? x3 (~10 net), THE UP SYSTEM x2 (~9 net), COMMAND x2 (~3 net). Total ~80 bytes reclaimed. Each factored word costs ~6 bytes overhead (CFA + threading).

summary: Convert apply-jbeam-hit to CODE word
description: apply-jbeam-hit is 68% primitives (mostly stack math, comparisons, variable access). Estimated ~117 bytes savings from ITC→assembly conversion. Handles Jovian beam hit resolution — damage application, shield interaction, system damage. — WON'T FIX: inlining rng/trig machinery into CODE costs more bytes than ITC threaded version saves. Not in hot path, so no speed benefit either.

summary: Convert beam-check-one-jov to CODE word
description: beam-check-one-jov is 66% primitives (collision detection math). Estimated ~66 bytes savings. Checks if player maser beam intersects a Jovian sprite bounding box. — CLOSED: word no longer exists in source, likely already converted or removed.

summary: Convert fire-jbeam-trace to CODE word
description: fire-jbeam-trace is 64% primitives (geometry, line tracing). Estimated ~82 bytes savings. Traces Jovian beam path from source to target, computing beam endpoints. — WON'T FIX: inlining rng/trig machinery into CODE costs more bytes than ITC threaded version saves. Not in hot path, so no speed benefit either.

summary: Convert proximity-damage to CODE word
description: proximity-damage is 64% primitives (distance math, damage calculation). Estimated ~82 bytes savings. Calculates splash damage from explosions based on distance to nearby objects. — WON'T FIX: inlining rng/trig machinery into CODE costs more bytes than ITC threaded version saves. Not in hot path, so no speed benefit either.

summary: Convert gen-spiral-arm to CODE word
description: gen-spiral-arm is 62% primitives (trig math, coordinate generation). Estimated ~60 bytes savings. Generates spiral boundary points for black hole event horizon rendering. — WON'T FIX: inlining rng/trig machinery into CODE costs more bytes than ITC threaded version saves. Not in hot path, so no speed benefit either.

summary: Convert ring-dot to CODE word
description: ring-dot is 61% primitives (circle geometry). Estimated ~54 bytes savings. Computes dot positions on explosion ring for animate-explosion. — WON'T FIX: inlining rng/trig machinery into CODE costs more bytes than ITC threaded version saves. Not in hot path, so no speed benefit either.

summary: Bridge station system — unified command input across views
description: Reframe damage report (cmd 1) and long range scan (cmd 3) as bridge stations (Engineering and Science). All non-combat commands work from any view. Pressing 1/3 toggles that station; pressing the other switches directly. Commands 5/6 auto-return to tactical before executing. Pressing 2 (warp) from Science is natural workflow. CLEAR/BREAK cancels command input only. Local combat pauses during overlays (thematic), background galaxy events continue. Replaces current any-key-dismisses-overlay behavior.

summary: Stars cluster at top/bottom of viewport — rnd-y uses non-power-of-2
description: rnd-y called 130 rnd, but rnd uses AND masking which only works for power-of-2 values. 130-1=129=$81, so only bits 0 and 7 were kept, producing just 4 possible y values (0,1,128,129). Stars plotted at y=5/6 (top) and y=133/134 (bottom) only. Fixed both rnd-x and rnd-y to use 128 rnd for correct uniform distribution.

summary: Jovian reinforcements can spawn on top of the Endever
description: do-spawn calls set-edge-pos which places Jovians at screen edges with random coordinates, but never checks proximity to the player ship. A reinforcement can land directly on the Endever, trapping the player. Fixed by retrying set-edge-pos if Manhattan distance to ship is < 10.

summary: Jovian spawns at left edge, appears on right side of screen
description: Reported twice: a Jovian reinforcement spawns at the left screen edge but visually appears on the right side. Possible causes: (1) sprite draw wrapping at VRAM row boundary if draw x goes negative, (2) unsigned arithmetic overflow in position centering, (3) movement code edge case. All movement code (jov-think, jov-flee, apply-intent) clamps to 4-123 x range. spr-draw and jov-spr-xy both clamp underflow to 0. Needs reproduction to diagnose further.

summary: Factor repeated code patterns to reclaim space
description: Several multi-word sequences are repeated 4-7 times in spacewarp.fs. Factoring into named words saves significant space: cancel-jbeam cancel-beam (x7, ~24b), draw-border draw-stars draw-storm-stars draw-event-horizon (x5, ~32b), SPR-MSL1 msl-px @ 1 - msl-py @ 1 - spr-erase-box (x4, ~60b). Total estimated savings ~100+ bytes.

summary: Factor cancel-jbeam cancel-beam into cancel-beams
description: cancel-jbeam cancel-beam appears 7 times. Factor into cancel-beams word. Estimated savings ~18 bytes.

summary: Factor draw-border draw-stars draw-storm-stars draw-event-horizon into draw-backdrop
description: 4-word backdrop redraw sequence appears 5 times. Factor into draw-backdrop. Estimated savings ~32 bytes.

summary: Factor pcol @ prow @ into here@
description: pcol @ prow @ appears 11 times. Factor into here@ word. Estimated savings ~40 bytes.

summary: Factor clamp-min patterns into 0max and 1max
description: DUP 0 < IF DROP 0 THEN appears 6x, DUP 1 < IF DROP 1 THEN appears 6x. Factor into 0max and 1max words. Estimated savings ~48 bytes combined.

summary: Move BG slot 1 to avoid gravity frame collision
description: BG slot 1 (jov-check-regen, check-dock, tick-dock, tick-base-attack) runs on odd frames where frame-tick AND 7 = 3, which collides with gravity frames causing 15,790cy (106% over budget). Moving to AND 7 = 7 avoids the collision: frame 1 drops to 14,430cy (under budget), frame 7 rises to 11,825cy (still under). Eliminates all over-budget frames.

summary: Factor msl-px @ 1 - msl-py @ 1 - into msl-coords
description: msl-px @ 1 - msl-py @ 1 - appears 7 times (lines ~635, 4218, 4671, 4813, 4827, 4833, 4842). Factor into msl-coords word. 8 words x 2 bytes = 16 bytes per call, replaced by 1 word = 2 bytes. Actual savings: 102 bytes (24,292 -> 24,190).

summary: Factor JOV-POS I 2 * + into jov-pos-addr
description: JOV-POS I 2 * + appeared 10 times. Factored into jov-pos-addr word. Actual savings: 86 bytes (24,533 -> 24,447). Original estimate was 42 bytes but each inline site was 14 bytes (LIT+val for both constant and 2), not 8.

summary: Factor SHIP-POS C@ SHIP-POS 1 + C@ into ship-xy
description: SHIP-POS C@ SHIP-POS 1 + C@ appears 5 times. Factor into ship-xy word. 6 words x 2 bytes = 12 bytes per call, replaced by 1 word = 2 bytes. Estimated savings ~44 bytes net.

summary: Factor SPR-MSL1 msl-coords spr-erase-box into erase-msl
description: Already factored — erase-msl word exists at line 4403. Closing as done.

summary: Self-destruct explosion draws over overlay if view changed during countdown
description: sd-detonate draws explosion graphics directly without checking overlay state. If player switches to damage report or scanner during the 5-second countdown, the explosion renders on top of the wrong screen. Fixed by adding dismiss-overlay at top of sd-detonate.

summary: Beam pixel artifacts — red remnants from Jovian beam contaminating ship bg save
description: Two issues fixed: (1) BEAM-PATH buffer overlap with JOV-SPR data — relocated to $8774/$89CC (prior fix). (2) Jovian beam-head red pixels persisted in VRAM across frames, contaminating save-ship-bg. When ship moved away, restore-ship-bg put red pixels back at old position. Fix: save-ship-bg zeros its 20-byte buffer when jbeam-total > 0, preventing beam-red from being captured as background. Stars under ship briefly show black during beam animation (~10 frames), invisible in practice. Cost: +16 bytes.

summary: Ghost Jovian beam after Jovian dies mid-fire — jbeam-pending not cleared
description: Partial fix: clearing jbeam-pending in cancel-jbeam addressed one race condition. Root cause was stale JOV-DMG data — see #298.

summary: Shield activation costs energy (command 4 redesign)
description: Command 4 now costs energy: 20 + level/5 (25%=25, 100%=40). Minimum level 25%. Rejects with NO ENERGY if insufficient. Includes deflector cap (#296).

summary: Shield-first damage absorption
description: Damage absorbed by shields first via take-damage helper. Overflow to random system via damage-system. Replaced complex percentage scaling. Unified JBEAM-DMG to 20 (removed JBEAM-SYS-DMG). Saved 32 bytes (24,234 -> 24,202).

summary: Regen prioritizes system repair over energy
description: repair-any checks systems in order (ion,warp,scan,defl,masr), +5% to first below 95%. Energy only regens when all systems healthy. 1 regen tick = 5% system repair.

summary: Death condition: all systems at 0% instead of energy depletion
description: Death check now: death-cause > 0 OR all 5 systems at 0%. Star collision sets death-cause=3 (new), black hole=1, self-destruct=2. Energy can reach 0 without death. Dock tick no longer exits early when energy=100 (continues repairing systems).

summary: Deflector health caps max shield level
description: pdmg-defl caps max shield level in command 4 handler. Implemented as part of #292.

summary: Remove passive shield drain from tick-energy
description: Removed shield drain branch and penergy=0 guard from tick-energy. Shields degrade from damage only. Saved 60 bytes (24,294 -> 24,234).

summary: Stale JOV-DMG in unused slots causes ghost beam and freeze
description: expand-quadrant only initialized JOV-DMG for active Jovian slots. Unused slots retained stale health values from previous quadrant. When tick-reinforcement bumped qjovians, pick-jovian found the stale slot alive and fired a beam from garbage position data, causing a permanent red line and freeze. Fix: clear all 3 JOV-DMG slots at quadrant entry.

summary: Jovian beam never hits ship — beam-find-obstacle truncates at ship sprite
description: fire-jbeam-resolve called beam-find-obstacle before beam-scrub-sprites. Ship pixels in the beam path counted as obstacles, truncating the beam before it reached the ship bbox check. Beams visually stopped at the ship edge but never applied damage. Fix: scrub sprites first, then find obstacles.

summary: Shield display not updating when taking damage
description: Panel only updated energy and missiles dynamically. Shield value (pshields) changed from beam hits but display never refreshed. Added prev-shields tracking and panel-val helper to update shields in real-time. panel-val also factored common pattern from update-energy and update-missiles.

summary: System repair stops at 95% — repair-any threshold too conservative
description: repair-any used 95 < threshold to avoid overshooting 100 with +5 increment. But systems at 95-99% were never repaired. Changed to 100 < since damage values keep systems aligned to multiples of 5.

summary: damage-system only hits ion engines and masers — 5 rnd AND-mask bug
description: 5 rnd uses AND masking (5-1=4=$04), producing only 0 or 4. Only ion engines (0) and masers (4) ever took system damage. Fixed with 8 rnd DUP 4 > IF 5 - THEN to map 0-7 into 0-4. Same class of bug as #276 (star distribution).

summary: Ship can protect starbase by intercepting Jovian beam
description: When Jovian fires at base and ship intercepts (jbeam-hit-ship set), base-attack timer resets. Player takes beam damage instead. Timer-based base destruction pauses while ship shields the base. Classic Trek tactic.

summary: Increase JBEAM-DMG to 75 with overflow to second system
description: JBEAM-DMG raised to 75. damage-system overflows remainder to a second random system when first system goes to 0. One unshielded hit damages two systems.

summary: Shields take 25 damage per hit instead of full damage
description: take-damage uses fixed 25 shield damage per hit. 100% shields last 4 hits. On shield failure, shields go to 0 (no overflow to systems).

summary: Shield bleedthrough below 40%
description: Deferred — needs more bytes. Will implement after further factoring.

summary: Ion engines at 0% disables movement
description: Check pdmg-ion in move-ship. At 0%, ship cannot move (sitting duck). Creates real consequence for engine damage and incentive to repair/dock. Also changed repair-any to fix ONE system per tick instead of all simultaneously — creates real repair queue pressure.

summary: Masers at 0% cannot fire
description: Removed 1max floor from maser-dmg. At 0% health, masers do 0 damage. Beam still fires visually but deals no damage. Full OFFLINE rejection deferred for bytes.

summary: Non-linear repair: field repair caps at 50-60%, no repair below 25%
description: Two thresholds: (1) Passive field repair cannot restore a system above 50-60% — full repair requires docking. Creates campaign attrition where you're never quite at 100% between bases. (2) Systems below 25% cannot be repaired at all without docking — heavy damage sticks. Combined with damage spread (#315) and permanent damage (#325), produces TOS endurance spiral. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md 'Field Repair' section.

summary: Scanner degradation at low health
description: Below 40% scanner health, LRS cells randomly show @ instead of real data. Scales with damage — 64 rnd vs pdmg-scan/2 per cell. At 0% every cell is garbled. Implements #149/#230. Cost: 54 bytes.

summary: Factor erase-msl, remove cancel-msl alias, trim check-spawn redundancy
description: Factored SPR-MSL1 msl-coords spr-erase-box into erase-msl. Removed cancel-msl alias (replaced with msl-kill). Removed redundant cancel-beams/msl-kill from check-spawn since refresh-after-kill already does them. Reclaimed bytes for damage redesign.

summary: Maser range-dependent damage — devastating close, weak at distance
description: Masers should be the primary weapon (the jab). Damage scales by distance from target: ~50 at melee (<15px), ~30 at mid-range (15-60px), ~10 at long range (>60px). Combined with direct hit bonus (#316), this rewards closing to engagement range. Makes masers complement missiles rather than being strictly inferior. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md 'Weapons Redesign' section.

summary: Missile damage nerf — no longer one-hit kill
description: Missiles currently do 100 damage (instant kill vs 100 health Jovians). Reduce to 60-80 damage so missiles soften but don't guarantee kills. Two missiles or maser+missile needed for a kill. Combined with finite base supply (#318) and smart evasion (#183), this makes missiles the finisher (the cross) not the primary weapon. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md.

summary: Jovian aim scatter by pilot skill
description: Before tracing a Jovian beam, offset the target point by (7 - pilot_skill) random pixels in x and y. A dolt (skill 0) scatters up to 7px and often misses at range. An ace (skill 7) fires dead center. Optional: additional 2-3px scatter when both ships moved this frame (moving target penalty). Creates TOS-like early-fight safety where unskilled Jovians barely threaten shields. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md 'Hit Accuracy' section.

summary: Damage spread across 2-3 systems per hit
description: Currently each unshielded hit damages 1 system (overflow to a second). Change to distribute damage across 2-3 random systems per hit. Creates the 'slow rust' attrition feel from the original Z80 version where the whole ship degrades gradually. Combined with shield bleedthrough (#306), this produces the TOS dramatic arc where the ship visibly falls apart. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md 'Key Differences' section 1.

summary: Direct hit bonus — center-distance damage scaling
description: When a beam hits a bounding box, measure pixel distance from hit point to target sprite center. Center hit = full damage + 'DIRECT HIT' status message. Edge hit = reduced damage (half or less). Applies to both Jovian and player fire. Overlaps with range damage (#312) — one calculation handles both: distance-to-center for accuracy bonus, distance-from-source for range falloff. Rewards precise angle entry. A direct hit to a critical system (deflectors/hyperdrive already at low health) could push it past 0% for extra dramatic effect.

summary: SOS timer system — replace random base destruction
description: Replace the current random 1-in-8 per-stardate base destruction with a deterministic timer. When a quadrant has both Jovians and a base, a destruction countdown starts (duration varies by Jovian count and difficulty). Arriving and engaging resets the timer. Supports escalating SOS messages (#319). The in-quadrant base attack mechanic (tick-base-attack) is already good — this fixes the off-screen heuristic. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md 'SOS and Base Defense' section.

summary: Finite base missile supply
description: Each base starts with 5-8 missiles. Docking restocks from the base's pool, not infinite supply. When a base's supply is exhausted it can still repair/recharge but not rearm. Base destruction loses remaining missiles permanently. Stored as 1 byte per quadrant (64 bytes in hi-mem, most zero). Creates campaign resource anxiety and makes base defense (SOS response) strategically critical. See COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md.

summary: SOS escalation messages
description: Condition line escalates through SOS-BASE, SOS-URGENT, SOS-FINAL as sos-timer ticks each stardate. On timer=3 base is destroyed with DESTROYED C,R notification. Extracted sos-label helper word to avoid nested IF/S" that triggers fc.py infinite loop.

summary: Spacebar quick-fire masers in arrow key direction
description: Spacebar fires masers in the direction of the currently held arrow key (no angle entry needed). Lower damage than aimed Command 5 fire (fixed mid-range equivalent). Enables XBlast-style dogfighting where arrows are both movement and weapon aiming. No direction held = no fire (prevents spam). Complements aimed fire for tactical variety.

summary: Status line micro-reports — TOS bridge crew flavor
description: Brief status messages on a dedicated panel row, auto-clearing after N frames: 'SHIELDS 80%' (after hit), 'DIRECT HIT' (center-of-sprite hit), 'EVASION' (Jovian dodges missile), 'SOS 3,6' (base under attack), 'REACTR LOW' (energy critical), 'SYS DAMAGE' (bleedthrough damaging systems). One status line, messages replace each other. Adds TOS bridge crew feel with minimal screen space.

summary: Crew count, casualties, and score
description: Ship starts with a crew count (e.g. 100). Hits that penetrate shields (bleedthrough or unshielded) cause casualties. Direct hits cost more crew than glancing blows. Crew partially replenished at bases. End-game score based on crew survival percentage. Crew count displayed on status/damage report. Adds core TOS immersion element — the ship has people aboard.

summary: Friendly fire — Endever weapons can damage starbases
description: Player masers and missiles that hit the base sprite damage or destroy the base. 'Don't shoot toward the base' becomes a real constraint during base defense. Beam-check and missile-check already use bbox collision; extend to include base sprite when present. Small code cost, significant tactical depth.

summary: Directional Endever sprites based on travel direction
description: 4 or 8 sprite variants for the Endever showing different orientations based on last movement direction. Each 7x5 variant = 5 bytes (4 variants = 20 bytes, 8 = 40 bytes in hi-mem). Also evaluate Endever sprite redesign — current triangle could be a small saucer+nacelle shape for better visual identity. Adds visual life to dogfighting.

summary: Permanent damage accumulation across campaign
description: Each hit that reaches systems adds 1-2% to a permanent damage floor that even docking cannot repair. Over a long game the ship gradually wears out. Keeps late-game tension high as even full-health systems can't reach 100%. Combined with field repair cap (#309) and finite base supplies (#318), creates the TOS endurance spiral.

summary: CODE words in kernel space — KCODE promotion
description: KCODE directive already existed in fc.py. Promoted save-jov-oldpos-n, jov-emo@, jov-emotion!, jov-emotion-base from CODE to KCODE — 150 bytes freed in app space, 180 bytes used in kernel staging area. More candidates: bg-save, bg-restore, jov-spr-xy, collision-scan.

summary: Combat system analysis document — original vs CoCo vs TOS
description: Created COMBAT_ANALYSIS.md comparing original Z80 Space Warp combat mechanics against our CoCo reimplementation, with detailed TOS tactical doctrine analysis ('The Kirk Test'). Covers damage distribution, shield models, repair, death conditions, weapon economy, and dramatic arc. Includes side-by-side tables, key differences analysis, and design vision for v1.0 combat overhaul. Priority summary table with byte estimates.

summary: Sprite rater — mobile-friendly evaluation web app
description: Built sprite_rater.py: zero-dependency Python web server for rating all 256 Jovian sprite seeds. Shows each sprite in 3 emotion colors, collects menace/quality/category ratings per seed. Mobile-first UI with keyboard shortcuts. Deployable locally, on Pi (--host 0.0.0.0), or AWS Lambda. Stores ratings in ratings.json. Documentation in SPRITE_RATER.md. Part of issue #189 sprite tuning effort.

summary: SVG sprite catalog — interactive HTML report with scatter view
description: Built gen_sprite_report.py: generates sprite_catalog.html with all 256 seeds rendered as inline SVG (no screenshot cropping). Three views: Catalog (filterable grid with menace/quality badges), Pages (8x8 grid matching CoCo display), Scatter (2D plot — menace vs quality axes). Sprite generation reproduced in Python from the 6809 PRNG algorithm. Uses sprite_data.py for descriptions and ratings. Part of issue #189.

summary: Screen capture MCP crash fix — use-after-free in non-ARC ObjC
description: Fixed crash in screen-capture-mcp C++ server. Root cause: SCShareableContent cache used raw ObjC pointers without ARC (project compiled without -fobjc-arc). Completion handler's autorelease pool could free SCWindow/SCDisplay objects while capture_window was iterating them. Fix: explicit CFRetain in completion handler, manual [content release] in each consumer function, serialized access with mutex. Removed 2-second cache (unnecessary for sequential MCP calls). Crash logs confirmed SIGSEGV in objc_msgSend from capture_window.

summary: Sprite test harness — sprtest.fs standalone Forth app
description: Built sprtest.fs: standalone Forth test app that exhaustively generates all 256 appearance seeds on the RG6 display. 8x8 grid, 64 per page, 4 pages per color pass (white/blue/red). Extracted gen-jov-sprite CODE word from spacewarp.fs as gen-test-sprite. Auto-advances with 8-second timer. Fixed: missing 'main' top-level call (required by fc.py entry point convention), I/J loop index swap, label/sprite overlap geometry. All 12 pages captured to archives/.

summary: Move screen CODE words to kernel (vsync, wait-past-row, count-blanking)
description: Move 3 screen sync primitives from forth/lib/screen.fs into kernel.asm. These are hardware-only (PIA $FF00-$FF03), no app dependencies. Add CFA symbols to kernel map so fc.py resolves them as kernel words. Remove CODE blocks from screen.fs. Est. ~82 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move rg-pixel CODE words to kernel (rg-pset, rg-line)
description: Move 2 RG6 pixel primitives from forth/lib/rg-pixel.fs into kernel.asm. Both use only kernel scratch vars (VAR_RGVRAM, VAR_LINE_*). General-purpose for any RG6 app. Est. ~429 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move sprite CODE words to kernel (spr-draw, spr-erase-box)
description: Move 2 sprite primitives from forth/lib/sprite.fs into kernel.asm. Both use only kernel scratch vars (VAR_SPR_*, VAR_RGVRAM). General-purpose for any RG6 sprite app. Est. ~369 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move rg-char CODE word to kernel
description: Move rg-char font rendering primitive from forth/lib/rg-text.fs into kernel.asm. Uses kernel config vars at $75-$7B (font base, glyph size, rows, bpr, row height). General-purpose for any text rendering. Est. ~75 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move beam CODE words to kernel (beam-trace, beam-draw-slice, beam-restore-slice, beam-find-obstacle, beam-scrub-pos)
description: Move 5 beam system primitives from forth/lib/beam.fs and spacewarp.fs into kernel.asm. All use kernel scratch vars (VAR_BEAM_*, VAR_LINE_*, VAR_RGVRAM). beam-find-obstacle and beam-scrub-pos are pure buffer operations. General-purpose for any beam/line-of-sight system. Est. ~779 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Add KCODE directive to fc.py for kernel-space CODE words
description: New compiler directive KCODE...;KCODE that assembles CODE words into the kernel region ($E86A-$FEFF) instead of app space. Requires second origin counter in fc.py, kernel binary patching, and FVAR_* resolution for app-space variable references. Enables moving game-specific CODE words (~4185 bytes) out of app space. Phase 2 after library words are promoted to kernel primitives (#332-#336).

summary: Deflector toggle redesign — UP/DOWN with key 4, no number entry
description: Replace numeric shield input (Command 4 + 3-digit value) with simple toggle. Key 4 toggles deflectors UP/DOWN. Shield strength = pdmg-defl (deflector system health). Eliminates pshields as a separate pool — deflectors ARE the shield. Simpler gameplay, more arcade feel. Removes energy cost for raising shields.

summary: Divert warp power to deflectors — recovery at 0% shields
description: When deflectors are at 0% (shields forced DOWN), pressing key 4 diverts warp power: transfers 25% from pdmg-warp to pdmg-defl and auto-raises shields. If warp < 25%, shows NO POWER rejection. Creates Trek-style risk/reward: survive now but lose escape capability. Scotty-approved.

summary: Repair priority based on deflector state
description: When deflectors UP: repair priority is deflectors first, then ion, warp, scanners, masers. When deflectors DOWN: repair everything else first, deflectors last. Creates tactical choice: shields up = shields heal, shields down = systems heal. All systems get +2% per tick.

summary: Panel display — DEFLCTRS nn / DEFL DOWN status
description: Replace SHIELDS nn display on panel row 17 with deflector state: DEFLCTRS nn when UP (showing pdmg-defl health), DEFL DOWN when shields are down. Update dirty-check to detect both pshields and pdmg-defl changes.

summary: Safe spawn refactor — unified pos-safe? word for all entity placement
description: Refactored into pos-safe? (addr -- flag) + safe-place (addr n --). Black hole now placed before base. All entities (base, Jovians, ship, reinforcements) use safe-place. Saved 70 bytes. Fixed base-vs-bhole check using uninitialized BHOLE-POS. Subsumes #233.

summary: Endever acceleration/deceleration — smooth movement ramp
description: Replace fixed-speed movement (currently 1-3px based on ion health) with acceleration model. pspeed variable (0-4) persists across frames. Key held: pspeed = MIN(pspeed+1, max_from_ion). Key released: pspeed = MAX(pspeed-1, 0). Ship moves pspeed pixels per frame. Ramp 0-1-2-3-4 over 5 frames gives analog feel. Deceleration on release = coast to stop. ~20-30 bytes for variable + ramp logic in existing move-ship CODE word.

summary: Panel layout — add left padding and proper alignment
description: Panel text is flush against left edge with no padding. Add 1-char left padding to both columns (left col x=0→1, right col x=17→18). Adjust value right-alignment columns accordingly. Full-width command line with terminal-style prompts (MASERS HEADING?, WARP QUADRANT?, etc). DEFLECTORS abbreviated to DEFLCTRS for fit.

summary: Cannot dock with starbase while shields are up
description: Shields up should prevent docking. Player must lower deflectors before receiving starbase assistance (energy recharge, repairs, missile resupply). Raising shields while docked also undocks. Trek logic: shields block all transfers.

summary: Missile stuck at 0 velocity for headings >= 720
description: sin() normalization only subtracted 360 once, so angles >= 720 read garbage past the 91-byte SINTAB. Could produce dx=dy=0, freezing the missile in place. Fix: use /MOD 360 for proper modulo normalization.

summary: Game crash — EXIT inside IF in repair-any
description: Game freezes with damage report remnants visible. Root cause: repair-any used EXIT inside nested IF/THEN (depth 2), which miscompiles in fc.py. Called every 16 frames via tick-energy and 3x via tick-dock, so crashes quickly when docked. Fix: replaced EXIT-based early return with stack flag pattern (DUP 0= IF DROP ... THEN).

summary: Death screen text formatting — stale chars and padding
description: Multiple text issues on death/win/lose screens: row 17 DESTROYED at x=0 instead of x=1, AGAIN? not clearing full-width COMMAND? prompt leaving 'D?' visible, stale 'D' from DESTRUCT countdown at col 0 causing 'DDESTROYED'. Also DEFLCTRS DOWN/UP transition leaving stale chars. Fixed: clear full row 17 from x=0, clear-cmd-area before AGAIN?, update-deflectors blanks before redraw.

summary: Merge beam/hit idle checks into single CODE word
description: Six beam tick words (tick-beam-erase, tick-beam-draw, tick-jbeam-erase, tick-jbeam-draw, apply-beam-hit, apply-jbeam-hit) each do a VARIABLE fetch + 0= + 0BRANCH just to early-exit when no beams are active. Merging into a single CODE word that checks beam-total, jbeam-total, and beam-hit-idx flags and dispatches only when needed. Estimated savings: 600-800cy. Low risk.

summary: save-ship-bg to CODE word
description: save-ship-bg is a Forth wrapper (837cy) calling bg-save CODE (140cy). The Forth wrapper costs ~700cy in ITC overhead — CONSTANT lookups, C@ fetches, arithmetic. Converting to CODE saves ~500cy. Low risk, small effort.

summary: Merge bg-jov orchestration into combined CODE
description: The full render path calls save-jov-oldpos (191cy Forth wrapper), save-jov-bgs CODE (900cy), and restore-jov-bgs CODE (909cy) sequentially. A combined CODE word that does oldpos+save or restore+draw in one pass could save 300-500cy by eliminating ITC call/return overhead. Medium risk — #248 was attempted before and reverted due to rendering bug. Needs careful testing.

summary: process-key gate check to CODE optimization
description: Attempted CODE latch-key using kernel KEY_SCAN (#380). Saved 11 bytes and ~500cy but caused display corruption — register or stack clobber undiagnosed. Reverted. Kernel KEY_SCAN subroutine is ready; app-side CODE word needs debugging.

summary: Redesign Endever sprite — chevron with twin red engines
description: Replaced boring triangle with shaped chevron: nacelle gaps, twin red engine pixels at stern. Blue hull, more character.

summary: Redesign starbase sprite — 7x7 ring with spokes
description: Expanded base from 5 to 7 rows. Blue ring with blue spokes, hollow center. Shifted QCOUNTS from $80B0 to $80B4 to avoid overlap with MSL2 sprite data.

summary: Fix MSL2 sprite invisible — QCOUNTS overlap after base resize
description: Expanding base sprite to 7x7 pushed SPR-MSL2 address into QCOUNTS shadow bytes at $80B0. QCOUNTS overwrote MSL2 data every frame. Fixed by moving QCOUNTS to $80B4.

summary: Dismiss overlay on reinforcement spawn
description: When Jovian reinforcements enter the quadrant while damage report or scanner overlay is active, clear the overlay before drawing to prevent visual corruption.

summary: Gravity through velocity — ship-gravity modifies velocity instead of position
description: ship-gravity @pull subroutine now nudges ship-vx/ship-vy instead of directly modifying SHIP-POS. Gravity feels like smooth drift instead of per-frame jerks. Saved 12 bytes by removing bounds clamping and moved-flag from @pull.

summary: Reduce missile flicker — move erase adjacent to draw, slow animation
description: Moved missile erase-msl right before draw to minimize CRT beam gap. Slowed sprite animation from every 2 frames to every 8 frames (RSHIFT 3). Missile reads as clean projectile with visible +/x alternation.

summary: Masers and missiles erase starbase sprite
description: Beam erase (paint black) and missile erase pass over starbase pixels, leaving permanent holes until next full quadrant redraw. Fixed by adding draw-base to both render paths (full and ship-only) so the base is refreshed every frame as part of the background.

summary: Gravity pulls wrong direction when velocity is zero
description: In @pull, LDB FVAR_ship_vx+1 between CMPA and BHI clobbered the Z flag. When velocity was 0, BHI tested Z=1 from LDB instead of Z=0 from CMPA, reversing the pull direction for below/right positions. Fixed by moving BHI before LDB.

summary: Ship erases stars on ship-only render frames
description: Stars were only redrawn on full render (jov-moved) frames. On ship-only frames, the ship bg restore/save cycle could leave stars erased. Fixed by adding draw-stars to ship-only render path.

summary: Gravity step too small for velocity system
description: Star and far-black-hole gravity used step=1, but velocity halving in move-ship zeroed it before it could be applied. Changed step to 2 so halving leaves 1 (still moves 1px per gravity tick).

summary: Reduce Jovian movement flicker on full render frames
description: Full render path has ~1,200cy gap between restore-jov-bgs (erase) and draw-jovians-live (draw). CRT beam catches the gap, causing visible flicker when Jovians move. Ship-only render is smooth. Potential fix: per-Jovian erase-save-draw cycle to minimize each sprite flicker window. Post-v1.0.

summary: Dock repair capped at 75% instead of 100%
description: tick-dock used repair-any which calls rep1 (capped at 75% by #309). Replaced with drep helper that repairs to 100%. Starbase docking now fully heals all systems as intended.

summary: SOS timer resets when target switches or threat clears
description: check-sos re-scanned every stardate, picking new targets and resetting timer when no threat found. Fixed: lock onto first threatened base, tick without re-scanning, only scan for new target after current is destroyed or saved.

summary: Win condition not triggered when last Jovian flees
description: jov-flee-to did not set check-win flag. If the last Jovian in the galaxy fled a quadrant, count-jovians would still find it elsewhere, but the check never ran. Fixed by setting check-win in jov-flee-to.

summary: Scanner column legend misaligned with data
description: Column numbers on long range scan were 1 character too far right. Changed x offset from 5 to 4 to align with data columns.

summary: Black hole reveal on death — explosion at BHOLE-POS
description: When the Endever flies into a black hole, explode-base animation now plays at the black hole position, revealing where it was. Previously only showed BLACK HOLE text with no visual indication of location.

summary: Maser visual noise at viewport edge
description: Beam drawing sometimes leaves artifacts when the maser hits the edge of the tactical viewport. Needs investigation. No room for fix at 4 bytes headroom.

summary: Maser kills do not trigger win condition
description: apply-beam-hit never set check-win flag. Only jov-kill (missile) and jov-flee-to set it. Centralized check-win in refresh-after-kill so all kill paths trigger the win check. Saved 16 bytes by removing redundant check-win sets from jov-kill, jov-flee-to, and destroy-base.

summary: Add U< kernel primitive
description: Unsigned less-than comparison. Standard Forth word, useful for boundary checks across all apps. Added to kernel as primitive #75.

summary: Add 0MAX kernel primitive
description: Clamp to >= 0. Promoted from spacewarp Forth definition to kernel primitive #76. Removed Forth definition, 6 call sites now use kernel primitive. Saved 22 bytes in app.

summary: Add 2* kernel primitive
description: Arithmetic shift left by 1. Added as kernel primitive #77. Used in jov-pos-addr (2 * → 2*), saved 4 bytes.

summary: Add WITHIN kernel primitive
description: Range test: ( n lo hi -- flag ). Standard Forth WITHIN using unsigned trick (n-lo u< hi-lo). Added as kernel primitive #80.

summary: Add 2/ kernel primitive
description: Arithmetic shift right by 1. Added as kernel primitive #78. Replaced 9 occurrences of 1 RSHIFT in spacewarp, saved 36 bytes.

summary: Add 0MIN kernel primitive
description: Clamp to <= 0. Mirror of 0MAX (#372). Added as kernel primitive #79.

summary: Allow energy drain to zero for weapons
description: Maser and missile fire required energy > cost (1+ extra). Now requires energy >= cost, allowing fire at exact cost and draining to zero. Removed 1+ guard from fire-maser and fire-missile. Updated cmd handler maser gate from 2 > to MASER-COST <. Saved 12 bytes.

summary: SOS base destruction clearing wrong quadrant
description: check-sos computed galaxy index as col*8+row instead of row*8+col. Base bit was cleared in the wrong quadrant, so destroyed bases persisted on the map. Fixed to use gal@/gal!.

summary: Stardate pace — 15 seconds per stardate
description: Changed STARDATE-FRAMES from 450 (~60s) to 112 (~15s). SOS bases now fall in 45 seconds (3 stardates). Creates meaningful time pressure for base defense.

summary: Stardate display not clearing previous digits
description: rg-u.r right-justifies without clearing the previous value. When stardate grows from 1 to 2+ digits, old digits remain on screen (9 -> 19 instead of 10). Also inconsistent end-col (15 vs 14). Fix: clear 3 chars at cols 12-14 before redraw, use end-col 15 consistently.

summary: Show coordinates when remote base is destroyed
description: When SOS timer expires and a remote base falls, display 'DESTROYED C,R' in the command area with the quadrant coordinates. Previously the base vanished silently from the map with no player notification.

summary: Add KEY_SCAN kernel subroutine
description: Extracted keyboard scan logic from CODE_KEY_NB into a KEY_SCAN subroutine callable via BSR/LBSR from CODE words. Returns ASCII in A, 0 if no key. Preserves X, U. Enables CODE words to scan keyboard without duplicating PIA logic.

summary: Warp non-operative below 25%, field repair recovers to 25%
description: Warp drive rejects commands when pdmg-warp < 25%. Field repair (rep1) changed from skip-below-25% to cap-at-25% — systems below 25% now repair up to 25%, then follow normal 75% cap. Ion drives unchanged (0% only). Cost: 18 bytes.

summary: Ghost ship sprite after warp
description: After warping to a new quadrant, if the ship moves on the first frame and a Jovian also moves, restore-ship-bg writes stale background data from the old quadrant. Root cause: do-warp did not sync old-sx/old-sy with the new spawn position or clear the moved flag. Fix: add save-ship-pos and 0 moved ! to end of do-warp.

summary: Gradual missile reload while docked — 1 every 500ms
description: Missiles now transfer from base to Endever one at a time while docked, every 30 frames (~500ms). 10 missiles in ~5 seconds. Replaced instant bulk transfer in do-dock with msl-reload frame counter in tick-dock. reload1 helper transfers 1 missile if player < 10 and base has supply.

summary: Relocate kernel variables from $0050 to kernel space ($EF03+)
description: Moved 50 bytes of VAR_* variables from $0050 (BASIC direct page) to end of kernel at $EF03+. Variables are now copied to all-RAM with the rest of the kernel by the bootstrap. Eliminates DECB record at $0050, making binaries LOADM-safe from Disk BASIC. All VAR_* accesses were already extended addressing — no performance change.

summary: Replace hardcoded kernel variable addresses with KVAR-* constants
description: fc.py now injects KVAR-* Forth constants from kernel.map (e.g. KVAR-CUR, KVAR-RGVRAM, KVAR-RGFONT). Replaced all hardcoded $0050/$0057/$0075-$007B references in spacewarp.fs, bounce.fs, sprtest.fs, tetris.fs, rain.fs, typewriter.fs, and rg-pixel.fs with symbolic KVAR-* names. Addresses resolve automatically when kernel layout changes.

summary: Dynamic kernel staging address in fc.py
description: Staging address for the kernel copy now packs right after the bootstrap ($0E19) instead of hardcoded $1000. Reclaims ~480 bytes of headroom before APP_BASE. fc.py patches the bootstrap's LDX source address and LDX #APP_BASE automatically. Prevents staged kernel overflow when KCODE words are appended.

summary: Space Warp APP_BASE lowered to $1F00 for LOADM compatibility
description: Changed --base from $2000 to $1F00 in spacewarp Makefile. App now ends at $7E5C instead of $7F5C, clearing Disk BASIC's stack area. Enables LOADM from disk without crashing. Works with both -run (direct) and LOADM (disk) loading.

summary: Top-level Makefile with DSK disk image target
description: New top-level Makefile: 'make' builds kernel + all demos, 'make dsk' creates build/demos.dsk with all 7 programs via Toolshed decb, 'make clean' removes artifacts. CoCo 8.3 filenames: BOUNCE, KALEIDSC, RAIN, TETRIS, RG-TEST, TYPEWRTR, SPACEWRP. Checks for decb on PATH with helpful install message.

summary: Version bump to V0.95
description: Title screen updated from V0.94 to V0.95. Reflects kernel variable relocation, LOADM-safe binaries, and DSK disk image support.

summary: Add version constant to Forth kernel
description: Define KERN_VERSION in kernel.asm (e.g. EQU $0100 for 1.0) so it appears in kernel.map. fc.py and downstream projects can read it. Print or embed version in hello world output.

summary: Add README and LICENSE to forth/
description: forth/ needs a top-level README.md covering: what it is, architecture (ITC, 6809), directory layout, build instructions, dependencies (lwasm, python3), hello world example. Add MIT LICENSE file. This makes forth/ a self-contained subproject that other repos (tiny-agent, space-warp) can reference.

summary: Verify forth/ is self-contained and builds independently
description: Ensure someone can clone just forth/ and build: kernel assembles, fc.py compiles hello.fs, combined.bin runs in XRoar. Check all paths are relative, no references outside forth/. Update any stale docs in kernel/README.md and tools/README.md.

summary: Hello world prints kernel version
description: Update hello.fs to display BARE NAKED FORTH followed by the kernel version (e.g. 1.0). Requires fc.py to expose KERN_VERSION as a Forth constant.
